I assume it's a bit louder inside the building itself, but not that the bass is thumping into the room until 1 a.m. so much that the bed vibrates. And quite honestly, I'm a good sleeper and very understanding, but... Such noise levels are unacceptable, especially given the hotel's policy of quiet hours from 10 pm to 6 am. After I asked for a discount, I was turned away rudely - according to the motto: I would have received an email and could have cancelled. My hairdryer in the room didn't work, neither did the access card to the room on Saturday, and I had to wait three-quarters of an hour at 10 pm to get into my room (no compensation was offered here either). The receptionists seemed more overwhelmed and annoyed than solution-oriented towards the guests. Keep your eyes open when choosing a job - I say this as a former hotel manager in what was then Berlin Mitte. I must commend the cleaning service. The lady was very helpful and also very friendly.

I cannot comment on breakfast and the restaurant - the bar was closed due to the parties. I hadn't booked breakfast - I wanted to have breakfast later in town.

I typically like a little bit of airflow while sleeping.

Very tasty meals in the afternoon buffet open from 4 p.m., we took advantage of it as a family - very large portions, tasty and relatively inexpensive.
neg_text:
Even though the facility is presented as a hotel and recreation/sports center, there is no gym and a PAID swimming pool also for hotel guests, PLN 24 for an adult and an additional PLN 12 for a swimming cap if you do not have one. The lady at the reception desk at the pool wasn't very nice, but I understand that someone can have a bad day.
Breakfasts rated as fantastic leave a lot to be desired. Since the breakfast restaurant was open until 11 a.m. on the weekend, one would expect that items would still be added to the buffet at 9 a.m. Unfortunately, nothing could be further from the truth, at 9:00 one inedible cold cut.
Some of the products were very tasty and fresh, but the buffet also included dried fruit and nuts in glass jars, as well as homemade jams and preserves, which looked very tempting - so my husband and I decided to take advantage of these benefits. Unfortunately, in nuts and dried fruit - the next day we found mold, which is an absolute disqualification for me.

The hotel is ok as a stopover, but the most disappointing thing is the breakfast, which is relatively expensive - for this price you can find a really good quality breakfast place in Wrocław, and in the city center.

The room is completely inconsistent with the offer. Instead of a King-size bed – a regular, small bed. The room looked completely different than in the photos – it was ugly, neglected, and full of dust. The worst thing, however, was the intense, unpleasant smell – reminiscent of sewers – which persisted throughout the stay and was difficult to bear. We also received a towel with holes.

The photos included in the offer were misleading – they showed a completely different, much better room. The hotel did not offer a room change or agree to a refund. I was forced to spend my entire stay there despite the terrible conditions.
After I asked the reception why I didn't get the room I paid for (approx. PLN 350 per night) The lady initially said that they no longer had these rooms available, but after a while she changed her mind and said that the photos were only illustrative. I wonder why the hotel doesn't put real photos on the booking page instead of illustrative ones, which are inaccurate anyway?

It is also worth remembering that cleaning is paid - PLN 25. The hotel does not include cleaning in the price. I didn't find this information on booking, but I read it at the reception desk.

I definitely do not recommend it - a huge disappointment and a waste of money.

This is the first time I've written a negative review about a hotel and I travel a lot. I'm sorry to have to write this, especially since I've used the hotel's services before and had no complaints.
On the day of check-in, we arrived at the hotel around 6 p.m. After receiving the keys and opening the room, we were immediately hit by the stench of the bathroom sewage system. We reported this fact immediately to the hotel reception but unfortunately all we heard was that there was no one to take care of it and that we would not be given another room. We were told that we could only write a complaint on the hotel form, which of course we did. I also asked for a refund, we wanted to stay overnight in another hotel but I was also told there was no possibility of receiving a refund.
To date, and some time has passed, we have not received a response to the complaint, and I would also like to mention that I have not received an invoice for the service (I reported this when booking the hotel). It's a shame that hotel guests are being ignored like this, because we would probably use the services of this hotel more than once, and after this situation we definitely won't do so and we advise others not to do so.

title: Peaceful, Nice, Clean, Pleasant
pos_text:
The meeting in Wrocław ran over, so instead of dreaming of the highway at 11 p.m., I ended up at the DIKUL Hotel. Last minute booking – and bingo! A huge room, impeccable cleanliness, and a receptionist who greeted you so kindly that you forgot about the whole day at work.

In the morning – the view from the window is worthy of an advertisement for Wrocław, even though the weather is rather “autumn depression”.

For comparison, I recently slept in a 5-star hotel, The Granary La Suite, for six times the price and… seriously? DIKUL wins with cleanliness and politeness of service.

To sum up: luxury is not just about gold door handles – sometimes it's just a good night's sleep and a sincere smile at the reception desk. I recommend!
neg_text: The bathroom lacks basic things – a toothbrush and toothpaste, shower gel, or even a little cream. When you make a spontaneous booking for one night, you really feel the lack of such details. The hotel has GREAT/HUGE/GIGANTIC potential, but it feels like it's missing the right direction. Looking at this hotel, I see a place that – with appropriate development and consistent management – can easily compete with premium facilities. All it takes is a little strategic approach to customer experience to make DIKUL your number one choice.

title: Jin Jiang - swept away
pos_text: I have a great fondness for the Radisson Hotel, as I was one of its first guests back in 2006. As the first 5-star hotel in Wrocław, Radisson has focused on the highest level of customer service from the very beginning. The rooms were spacious, comfortable, and the service so exceptional that every night spent there remained in the memory as something truly special. This experience shaped my expectations of luxury and quality of service for a long time – and I still remember it with great fondness.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, today the hotel no longer represents the same standard. It is clearly visible that the facility is neglected and the staff expects guests to treat the hotel as if it were a former top 5*, even though times have changed and many much more exclusive facilities have been built in the area.

Management remained stuck in the Radisson glory days, failing to keep up with new trends in hospitality. Unfortunately, it is also clear that the entire network is conservative and resistant to change. As a result, today's Radisson hotels have largely ceased to be synonymous with 5* luxury, becoming rather a chain for corporate clients organizing conferences, offering a correct standard, but far from the former excitement and exceptional guest experience.

title: Wroclaw 1 night
pos_text: Massage. It's hard to find anything more.
neg_text: Complete drama. I don't really know who gave it 5 stars. From the very beginning (basically before your stay) problems started… The massage appointments had to be rescheduled half an hour earlier because even though the spa is open until 10pm, the massage had to end at 9:30pm, which means I had to cut the massage in half because We came from the route straight for a massage. The lady at the reception, Maria or Daria, couldn't quite cope with the invoice. And I was informed that I would not receive an invoice, only when I asked to contact the manager, the invoice was made in 45 seconds. But it was possible! The rooms show signs of time, but are relatively comfortable. We were there in a very hot weather, so the air conditioning was not coping well. The breakfast was quite modest (it's hard to say it's five-star - I recommend going to a five-star hotel and checking what they serve). The same lady at the reception who refused to issue an invoice told me that we had to use the parking ticket until the end of our trip. Upon departure, it turned out that the ticket did not work. There was a traffic jam behind us (the parking lot is so narrow that it's difficult for a larger car to pass), despite the information that we should call the phone number, no one answered. We had to go to the reception and wait again, because when there is checkout in the morning, one slow person is enough. To sum up, the hotel might not have left such bad memories, but the service at every stage was dramatic. To be replaced. We didn't just use the restaurant… I'm afraid of what we would "find" there 🤣 if you have the option of choosing another hotel, even with four stars, I recommend choosing 4 stars.

Dog wandering around in breakfast in the morning - barking and yelping, at least the hot water was back so he probably washed his paws.

title: Not a 5-star hotel, more like a 3-star hotel!
pos_text: Large room
neg_text:
We arrived at the hotel a little earlier (1 hour) due to a cancelled client appointment. Check It was not possible; we were left sitting in the lobby for an hour with the explanation that the rooms were not ready.

When we saw the room, we wondered what had been done to clean it. (Pictures)
Dust in the corners and long black hairs in the sink.

The wellness area is very outdated; there are ant nests in the relaxation area.
When asked, staff said that they had already informed management, but nothing would be done (see pictures). One can also see from the picture of the sauna rules that things are being done extremely carelessly here.
When I asked to speak to the manager, I was informed that he was not in the building.
Shortly after my request, the ant nests were vacuumed up, but two hours later they were back!

The sauna is supposed to be usable until 10 pm. We were there at 3 pm, everything was fine, the sauna temperature was 90 degrees. When we wanted to go for another sauna session at 7 pm, the sauna heater was already switched off, and the sauna only had a temperature of 60 degrees!

title: big disappointment. the hotel was unfairly rated so high
pos_text: Beds - large, comfortable, we really slept well, which is a plus. And I guess that's all.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, I have a lot of reservations:
1. First and foremost - I am allergic, I chose this hotel primarily for the sauna, because I really need it during this period. At the reception I was informed that the sauna is available from 7 a.m. I was there at 8:30. the wellness area was not cleaned, the saunas were cold, and the jacuzzi too. I had to leave after an hour to make it to the meeting. By this time the sauna had heated up to 40 degrees, so I was unable to use this facility. Around 9:00 a.m. the cleaning lady came, washed the windows, and scrubbed the floor with a bucket. I understand that such things are the norm in public swimming pools, but not in a hotel of this class in the wellness area (!). You can't relax in such conditions. A huge minus and this situation alone makes me regret my choice and if I had to choose a hotel again, I would definitely not choose this one.
2. Breakfast was mediocre, typical hotel breakfast, weak coffee, dry cakes. There was prosecco available at the entrance, it looked like the staff were going to pour it but they probably didn't feel like it, it was in a cooler with water - melted ice, it was warm. The tables were not cleaned, we could feel crumbs from the previous guests under our hands. The gentleman from the service suggested dishes to order. We ordered poached eggs - they were prepared incorrectly, with way too much vinegar, it was impossible to eat. We went out to the terrace for coffee and dessert, but it was not cleaned up there either, and the umbrellas were not open despite the strong sun.
3. The service here was also a huge disappointment, unfortunately. I arrived with a pregnant woman, no one asked if they could help us, no one held any doors, no one even showed the slightest interest in us. When leaving, we were informed about the cost of 80 PLN for parking, which we had not received any information about beforehand, the person at the reception did not even ask how our stay was, basically took our cards, issued an invoice and said "Goodbye" without even a smile, anything, as if we were making a problem of taking advantage of the offer.

A hotel full of contradictions. On the one hand, the service is very pleasant and helpful, but on the other hand, in contrast, some elements are very weak.
Inflexible standards within the hotel, and it all started with getting an invoice. Then breakfasts, which are very good, but end at 10:00, with the emphasis on end. The curtain is drawn and the staff eats breakfast. This is the first time I have encountered such a custom, usually hotels are flexible with their guests. Moreover, on Sunday there is not even a moment longer for breakfast, because the curtain is drawn again, where the food is placed and the staff eats. A very strange standard, especially with that curtain. It looked as if the food was to be left for the hotel staff to prevent the guests from eating it all. There is no longer any breakfast on Sunday. On the last day there was a wedding reception until the morning and the morning staff was extremely tense and tense. The staff said things to us that should never have been said to a customer. The words and tone of voice were unacceptable at all, and even more so in a 4* hotel. After check in I was asked if the room should be cleaned, I confirmed that it was. Unfortunately, it wasn't, and we weren't brought any water to make up for the lack. It was only done at my request. For such a high price for accommodation I would expect a better standard. I recommend the hotel, but please work on the hotel standards, because it is 4*.

title: Given a small room we did not book, refused an upgrade or compensation, terrible customer service!!
pos_text: The only saving grace was this room appeared clean.
neg_text:
My partner and I decided to treat ourselves and booked one of the larger suites at Hotel Dikul. However, upon arrival we were checked into an small double room just off reception on the ground floor. It was clearly a room
for people with accessibility issues. The room was dated and, compared to the suite we booked, was considerably smaller. It looked nothing like the suite advertised on booking.com’s site. I checked my booking to confirm that I had in fact booked the other room, and I had. We went to reception to request a room move. We were told there were no other rooms. I asked to speak with the manger… they weren’t around. The person on reception looked very young and was extremely unhelpful. I was told that was the room I had booked, which it clearly wasn’t. There was no acknowledgment or apology whatsoever. I asked for a refund if I wasn’t able to stay in the room I had booked. All I was given was a complaints form to hand write. Thankfully our stay was only for 1 night. Upon checkout we were asked if we enjoyed our stay. We explained the situation to the new receptionist who proceeded to sit silently and stare at us. An apology really would have gone a long way. All I can think and hope is that they aren’t used to people not enjoying their stay, which may be reassuring to others I guess. For us the customer service was truly terrible. A week or so after our stay I was emailed a discount offer for my next booking at hotel dikul!! Needless to say I would never ever stay there again. Also to note that the hotel isn’t actually on the market square. It’s close to the market
Square and is still in a convenient location, but we felt the name on here is a little misleading.

Parking is available according to the offer, but on site it turns out that you have to book a space in advance, so there is no space. Overall a failure. Bad experiences. title: Bad
pos_text: NOTHING
neg_text:
I wholeheartedly recommend this hotel if you don't want to be disappointed like we were. We arrived exhausted after traveling halfway across the country to the Christmas market. We had specifically booked a king room to have more space, but what we found there left us stunned. The photos presented on the website have no relation to reality, it's not about a different furniture arrangement or a differently made bed - THE ENTIRE ROOM WAS 3 CLASSES BELOW WHAT WE SIGNED FOR. We were greeted by an unpleasant smell, the entire space was filled with rickety furniture, and on the bed were two unmade duvets covered in dust and HAIR! There was also hair all over the scratched floor. The string of the large roller blind was broken, so the only thing covering the windows were dirty curtains. The walls were scratched and scuffed, and in the bathroom we were greeted by cobwebs and a spider on them.

The longer we stayed there, the more we felt cheated and finally we decided not to stay in this hotel any longer, so we reported it to Booking and moved to the JAZZ hotel, 4 minutes away, where we actually felt like we were in a hotel, and the illustrative photos are not misleading.

THIS IS DEFINITELY NOT A 200 PLN PER NIGHT HOTEL, AT LEAST 50 PLN.

I will just add that the only thing we have recovered so far is PLN 60 and the photos showing the King room on the hotel booking page are photos of a 4-person room. Below I am attaching the actual appearance of the King room of the "hotel" dikul ;)

Bad smell from the bathroom, barrier - free again, dirty sheets, dirt on the floor, dirty remote controler from AC. We decided to leave the hotel, because it was not really what we ordered. We asked for parking refund, we parked only for 1 - 1,5 hour. There was another lady, maybe they changed shifts, so I informed her again about the problems. She told me that she is new employee so she doesn’t know what to do with the refund, so she called some colleague to help her. The summary was that unfortunately she cannot refund and gave me only some kind of complaint form, which I filled of course. I asked for a manager but there was no one on site, then I asked for manager email so I can contact her/him,but I received only a hotel email, which is not valid in such a situation.
The result is, that the hotel is ignoring my e-mails, no one is responding and every time I call them they tell me that manager is not working. I donť know, but this is just not professional at all.
I am badly dissapointed, because they are not able to even refund me for the parking! I know I paid for non refundable reservation but in such a matter you should fully refund. I am also attaching real photos.

We reported that the sink handle was spinning almost 360 degrees and needed to be repaired, and it was also impossible to take a shower without flooding the bathroom. After just 2 minutes, water was pouring out of the shower (you can see from the photos that it's actually a walk-in shower, so the slope isn't right, it floods everything) and it was impossible to keep the water running for even 5 minutes. Hostel-like sheets, small and uncomfortable pillows, and there were no bathroom products (shampoo or shower gel). I think paying almost 150 euros a night to then have these terrible services is unacceptable. If you find a room for 60 euros a night, that's fine...but if not, avoid it and go somewhere else. title: Bad smell, stuffy, dysfunctional bathroom
pos_text:
Breakfast - large selection, lots of extras, lactose-free milk, quality meats and cheeses. There is plenty to choose from.

There is no air conditioning or any ventilation in the rooms, and on top of that there is an unpleasant smell inside...

Opening a window doesn't do much good when it's warm outside. A stay of several days in August was absolutely unbearable, the room was almost suffocating from the stuffiness. We ran away from it immediately after breakfast, because on top of everything else there was the nasty, musty smell of the old room.

The bathroom ventilation only worked for a minute or two after being turned on (so you would have to turn it on every now and then), but it didn't really give anything anyway. The toilet is cramped and uncomfortable.

The shower and sink drains are severely clogged.
The water pressure in the shower head, despite different times of showering, was constantly very, very weak, it was difficult to rinse the shampoo out properly.

No sockets by the beds, TV smaller than the advertising screens in supermarkets. Not that we need it, but it does complete the picture.

The cleaning crew does their job well, and the cleanliness is generally okay, but the facility is definitely showing signs of age and really needs a refresh.

This could've been mitigated properly if the staff would've answered our initial booking message in which we specifically asked if they have problems with sound insulation and if they did, we could've cancelled our booking 4 months in advance. But they didn't respond at all. Furthermore, when confronted with the sound issues and the fact that we didn't sleep during the night and that we want to leave immediately, the staff didn't handle the situation promptly and wasted more of our time. title: Hotel for the patient. The hotel's 3 stars are a misunderstanding. Not for this money.
pos_text: On the plus side, the location is great (11/10), perfect for exploring the city on foot. Tasty breakfasts, quite a wide selection, good coffee. Elevator. Aesthetically decorated hotel. The bathroom is fine.
neg_text: At first glance, it might seem that the hotel is OK, but the details "put it down" my stay. When I got there, it turned out that I was not assigned the room I requested (not facing the street), in addition, a broken fan was constantly humming in the room and could not be turned off. Here everything was saved, because they changed my room - not facing the street and with a working fan (or so I thought, because later it turned out that, for a change, it was hardly blowing at all). It seemed that it would be 9-10/10 now, unfortunately what immediately caught my attention was the internal door in my room, as you can easily guess - connecting my room with another one. While the first night the adjacent room seemed empty, unfortunately on the second night, as expected, the loud family watching TV around 1 a.m. of the neighbors outside the door prevented me from sleeping properly. Perhaps it wouldn't have been audible if our rooms had been separated by a wall, but unfortunately, as we've already established, it was the door :). Luckily they were at least closed,.. Since it was the last night of my stay, I gave up on another conversation with the staff, but what kind of standard is this at this price in a hotel? A completely incomprehensible situation. I booked in advance. In turn, the previous day, when I was returning from sightseeing, my room card suddenly stopped working and they simply replaced it without a word of explanation? Poor communication from staff. The gentleman didn't speak to me at all, while one of the ladies spoke so quickly and unclearly that I had trouble understanding her... Are guests a sad necessity? It is also worth mentioning the wonderful green "wardrobes" for clothes that are so narrow that the clothes have to stick out through those funny "windows" like Nussbeisser chocolate-covered nuts.

title: No clue why this hotel is highly rated on Booking
pos_text: The location is great. The rooms inside were clean.
neg_text:
- MOST IMPORTANTLY: the AC DOESN'T WORK PROPERLY; we were there when the temperature outside was about 26-27 degrees C (so not even as high as it gets), but it was practically impossible to have the temperature in the room get below 24 degrees C; as you may see in the point below, it seems to be the case for all the rooms; we ended up swapping hotels after one night because of it
- the stuff: while the stuff was very kind and helpful in standard situations (showing us to the room, check-in, etc.), when there appeared an issue (AC not working), they were not helpful and quite unprofessional; when we complained about the A/C, one staffer told us that ""it was normal that the temperature in the room could not be further decreased, as the day was sunny and the windows in the room were very large", another said that "the A/C system is just different from other hotels", yet another offered us a different room and after our requests and inquiries has assured us that she's verified it personally and that the other room definitely had a properly working AC, so my husband has packed all our things and moved them to that room (on a different floor) just to discover that in that room the A/C had an even bigger problem: it was not even possible to turn it on;
- cleanliness: although the rooms were clean, the corridor leading to them and the lift were not; the carpets were very stained
- room setup: there was this strange setup where the bathroom was divided from the room by a glass wall; we didn't understand the idea to begin with (as probably it's also more costly to furnish), however, it could be done in an acceptable way (that we've seen in a different hotel) with adding a light-blocking curtain to the setup.

title: Basic but nice.
pos_text:
The highlight of my stay was undoubtedly the hotel's exceptional customer service. The staff members were not only friendly but also went out of their way to assist with any inquiries or requests I had. Their genuine willingness to help created a welcoming atmosphere that enhanced my overall stay.

The cleanliness of the hotel was another notable aspect. The attention to detail in maintaining a spotless environment did not go unnoticed. Whether it was the lobby, the corridors, or my room, everything was impeccably clean, contributing to a sense of comfort and well-being.

The comfort of the bed was exceptional. After a day of exploring the city, sinking into the plush mattress was a blissful experience. The quality of the bedding and pillows added to the overall coziness, ensuring a restful night's sleep.
neg_text:
One of the disappointments was the value for money concerning toiletries. The limited selection provided in the room did not align with the hotel's pricing. Guests typically expect a more extensive array of toiletries, and this aspect felt lacking in comparison to other establishments.

Another inconvenience was the absence of an ironing board in the room. While the hotel may offer an iron upon request, having this basic amenity readily available would have added to the convenience of the stay.

The quality of the hairdryer provided was below expectations. A more professional-grade appliance would have been appreciated, as the one in the room seemed outdated and less effective than anticipated.

Furthermore, the inability to connect personal devices using an HDMI cable due to the unavailability of an adapter was a frustrating limitation. In an era where connectivity is crucial, this oversight could be a significant inconvenience for guests who rely on such amenities.

title: Great location but the hotel itself was a bit of a disappointment
pos_text: Very good location, nice and helpful staff, quite well soundproofed, comfortable bed.
neg_text: Very cold room: unable to adjust room temperature to suit your needs - unfortunately, despite increasing the heat level the temperature still remained the same - very cold. Blackout curtains. No wardrobe/instead of a wardrobe, a few hangers and 3 small, completely darkened shelves. The bathroom is literally in the room, separated only by a smoked glass window and a curtain, which means you can hear everything from there. The shower was open to the bathroom, literally flooding it, the water was so heavily chlorinated that the smell of chlorine lingered in the room for a very long time, and at the same time there was no way to air the room. No possibility to use the offer of streaming services despite the information provided on Booking.com

title: A Beautiful Object with Wasted Potential
pos_text: Location, decor, architecture of the facility
neg_text:
Beautiful facility and location, but I have the impression that everyone works there as a punishment. Well, maybe apart from the lady at the reception who welcomed us and the gentleman from room service. The organization in the restaurant is completely broken. Every second thing was missing at breakfast, the staff was wandering around the room with grimacing faces, not noticing that basic products were missing from the buffet. Drawing attention causes reactions, but with great grace, and even at that moment there is no smile on the faces. The preparation time for a meal from the breakfast menu was so long that we lost our hunger.

I would like to emphasize that the problem does not concern one waiter or waitress. The atmosphere was as if someone had just been killed, and what's more, it was probably the hotel guests who were the killers.

If a fish rots from the head down, then the head is in a state of complete decomposition.

Hotels in large cities fall into two categories.
1. It was great, next time I won't look for anything else.
2. It was okay, but I definitely won't be back. but how to do it to remember it.

This is the second category.

NOTE: The rooms have bathroom architecture that requires a couple traveling together to be in a state of mind where they can use the toilet next to each other. This may be a problem for some.

Aaa... and to this day I don't know what it means"The poached eggs are gone but they are still fried" in the context of a meal made fresh and ordered from the menu. What ended there? Water in the pot? Well, it's an egg if it can be fried.

pos_text: Good location. Breakfast included in the price is tasty and with a large selection of dishes.

I requested the possibility of reserving a parking space in the reservation form (paid parking) and I indicated that I would arrive between 2:00 p.m. and 3:00 p.m. (the hotel check-in time is 2:00 p.m.). I received a reply via email that the staff would be happy to fulfill my "special request". Upon arrival, I was informed that the hotel does not take parking reservations, and the staff stated that my "special request" was to check in at 2:00 PM (!?). I will just add that there were a dozen or so spaces in the hotel parking lot with the description - "reserved space". This is some kind of misunderstanding. But eventually a parking space was found and that is good news. But what's the point of these "special requests" if, as a rule, they are not fulfilled and regular check-in during standard hours is treated as special treatment of guests.

title: Excellent staff, modern, central hotel but poor sound insulation for Friday DJ sets.
pos_text:
Modern and nicely designed hotel with very friendly, supportive and professional staff. Central location and paid parking on site - which
made arrival very convenient. Good communication on the phone too.
neg_text: What put a dent in the one night stay was the Friday DJ set (organized by the hotel itself) that ended at 22.00. We had a room on the 3rd floor with windows facing the hotels yard and the DJ set was so loud, that even with closed windows you could just hear loud music in the room. Not just the bass even, the set. As someone who was looking to rest up in the evening after the day of travel - needles to say I was upset about but a) too tired to go downstairs and make this my problem b) the set wouldn’t be stopped just because one person can’t rest up. I informed reception next day that it really wasn’t ok.

title: Weekend trip to Wroclaw
pos_text: The bed was comfortable. The hotel was in a great location, there are some lovely vars and restaurants nearby and only a few minutesxwalk tontgexsquare. The decor was pleasant and tge hotel was clean. There is a beautiful garden area which is very peaceful. The staff were very friendly and helpful. There was a good selection for breakfast. Great to have free use of the drinks machine in the reception area.
neg_text:
Everything in the room is controlled by a tablet, in the first room we went in the tablet did not work properly so we had no air conditioning and we were extremely hot in the night. The staff were very helpful and we changed rooms which was better. The air conditioning definitely worked better (especially on night 3) however the bathroom underfloor heating didn't work in either room meaning the floor of the toilet and sink area stayed wet too.
Even the do not disturb function didn't work on either tablet, we just got an error message.

title: It was impossible to rest during the day, I wouldn't have chosen this accommodation if I had known that the employees came to chat
pos_text: The room was nice, breakfast was okay.
neg_text:
There is a renovation elevator at the entrance. The room was nice, BUT: - one coffee for two people, - no spoon or stick to stir the coffee, - the toilet paper was transparent, not even suitable for blowing your nose, and there was nothing else, - two body towels, but what to wipe your hands with? Or where is the foot towel or rug?
- the room equipment set mentioned on the card on the door was missing, even though the cleaning staff had a supply of it (we saw it in the corridor).
- no information that the hotel is under renovation. As a guest, I came to the hotel to rest after my trip and the whole time I was listening to drilling in the walls literally next door. Additionally, as someone who struggles with migraines, it didn't help me. If there had been information about this, I would not have chosen this accommodation.
- breakfast was okay, but it's a shame that several ladies were chatting away at the reception desk, and there was no one to bring glasses or cutlery, or even clear the tables, lemon in "lemon water" also missing apparently. There was no milk for the cereal, nor for the coffee machine. You had to go around looking for the lady and ask her to do what she was paid for. Staff to be trained. We had to sit at a table that, although there were no plates left after the guests, was covered in crumbs and splashed with drink because it was the only table not as cluttered as the rest.
- very low quality meat, I'm not surprised there's so much of it left. The chairs in the dining room are all scratched up, the fabric is peeling, their glory days are behind them.

title: Ok for one night

neg_text: The rooms are small, the neon colors are terribly irritating, the milk powder is old - it curdled in the coffee, the bathtub is damaged - I added another room, paid parking, hard beds, overall the hotel needs a little renovation.

title: good hotel only for one night because the next days you forgot
pos_text: the position
neg_text:
the hotel's philosophy (ADD IT TO THE SITE) which, under the pretext of adhering to an environmentalist plan, did not tidy up, clean or change the linen for four days (even though the website states daily cleaning). On the third day, in the evening, when I asked the reason, at reception I was told that the cleaning of the room and the change of bathroom linen (two shower towels and one hand towel for two people), although included in the price, was only upon request due to the hotel's participation in an energy saving plan and that they would take care of it the following day (they hand-delivered me two shower towels and only one hand towel which was to be used by two people). The next day (the fourth) the room was neither cleaned nor tidied and following my complaints to the floor cleaner, the room was cleaned and tidied in the late afternoon. These features of the hotel services (absurd because I can understand the change of linen, but what energy savings does the tidying of the room refer to?). Uncomfortable bed with springs that are barely supportive.
The staff at the reception, never a greeting or a smile

title: Okay for a short stay!
pos_text:
The hotel has modern furnishings. The rooms are also functional. The beds are comfortable, but very worn out.
Free parking is available in front of the hotel, if one is free. We had parked on the other side of the Oder river.
neg_text:
The hotel staff are young, but not very hardworking. Many people showed a lack of enthusiasm for work, and they also left their friendliness at home.
The breakfast buffet was no longer fully replenished even one hour before the end. The staff had to be reminded about many different missing items (e.g., cups). It was also unpleasant that, for example, spilled jam wasn't removed from the buffet!
Dinner consisted of a starter and a main course. This is quite a surprise, as there was no map to be seen anywhere!
On the first evening we were informed that water, apple juice and/or orange juice would be included at no extra charge. On the second evening, we were simply given water, and on the third evening there was nothing at all. We weren't even asked!
It was also strange that the restaurant was closed on Sundays. When we inquired about dinner, we were informed that dinner would be provided for guests who had already booked half-board. When we sat at the table, a small salad was placed in front of us. When asked again if that was all, the answer was that there were no waiters today and that this was all that was available in the kitchen. And actually, nobody really knows for sure.

title: Location is ok and that's all
pos_text: The location is very good, parking is paid, but we got a space without any problems. The appearance of the room is correct, standard. A very nice view.
neg_text:
Breakfasts are served until 10:30, but unfortunately there was nothing on the plate at 9:30. Literally three mini rolls, no cheese, no lettuce and many other things. The waitresses, instead of checking what was missing, preferred to giggle in the kitchen that, as a great favor, after being noticed, they started clearing the tables that should have been cleared long ago after the guests had left. The lady didn't even wipe our table, it was sticky, there were crumbs all over the table. I think that the ladies have absolutely no idea even the basics of customer service. Breakfast was very modest, with very little variety. Moreover, to make coffee or tea, we have to stand behind the heads of the guests who have a table next to us. Very uncomfortable. Dangerously. The coffee machine is placed in a very bad location. Same thing with juices. I had to apologize to other guests if I could pour juice so as not to disturb them while they were having breakfast, the juices were placed high, almost above their heads. The first section of tables and chairs is ok, but in the back of the room there are armchairs whose glory days have long since passed. It's a shame that guests have to sit on armchairs whose leatherette is falling off and is all cracked. The restaurant is closed on Sundays. However, the nice bartender suggested the dish of the day, because the hotel was serving the group, otherwise it would not have been possible to eat anything in the restaurant. We waited a very long time for the dish of the day, namely: cream of tomato soup - be careful with penne pasta, and for the second course the same penne pasta (overcooked, to boot) with chicken and spinach. Poorly.
In addition, the heating kept turning off and the radiator in the bathroom was always cold. A bathtub with a glass cover that was broken. One can of still water in a double room. Tea and coffee, sugar and creamerfor coffee in the amount that the cat cried, in a room for two days for two people.

There was hair everywhere, and the shower had several black spots in the grout that looked like mold. The towels smelled musty and were completely frayed.
I would not recommend booking this hotel. The rooms were also not cleaned in the morning, as is usually the case.

title: Two banks
pos_text: Location, good breakfast
neg_text: Of the several hotels in Wrocław on the selected date, Dikul was chosen. Of course, I was guided by the photos of the rooms and I didn't even notice that there were no photos of the bathroom anywhere. Location, cleanliness in the room, breakfast ok. Unfortunately, after entering the room, consternation and surprise because the factual situation and photos are two different stories. The room was dark, as if it had come straight from the communist era, and the bathroom looked like a hospital bathroom from the communist era, the only thing missing was a white stool on metal legs. The nice lady at the reception told me that unfortunately all the rooms look like this, including the apartment offered. Yes, I think the photos were generated by AI or the owner's wishful thinking. I know there is competition on the market, etc., but I believe that honesty towards customers comes first. I suggest posting real photos of the rooms and bathrooms so that all customers can make an informed decision and not be unpleasantly surprised on the spot.

The latter is not a bad thing in itself, however, apparently all rooms are like that except for a few that are pictured on the portal. However, it was not clear to us which category they belonged to when we selected our rooms. Our booked king-size bed turned out to be two single beds pushed together with hard mattresses and rustling bedwetting protectors…
Considering the room price, I found the parking fee of €15 per night to be relatively high.

Dust was constantly falling from the ventilation system above the bed. The toilet brush had already seen better days. The refrigerator only works when you are in the room! Few electrical outlets and no bedside table for the person sleeping outside. TV only had Polish channels - unfortunately, battery acid leaked from the remote control!
There is a massive rat infestation in the neighboring building (rear entrance to the hotel and parking lot)!

title: OK, but with room for improvement
pos_text: Close to the old town - it's just a few meters on foot to the square in front of the town hall.
neg_text: The bathroom was very clean, but it smelled extremely unpleasant, like drain water. Because the door was open, the entire room smelled bad upon entering.

title: Passable
pos_text:
The location is very good; the city center can be reached in a few minutes on foot. There is also a hotel parking lot for a fee, which was okay.
Breakfast was also very good.
The room is spacious and quiet.
neg_text:
The refrigerator wasn't working. A lamp by the bed is also not acceptable. There was also a safe that couldn't be used.
We reported it immediately on the day of arrival, it was noted - but nothing happened.
The next day I complained again - the refrigerator was replaced and the lamp was fixed.
The room was not cleaned.
The staff at reception were generally uninterested and unfriendly.

title: Great location and that's all, what a pity.
pos_text: The location and parking right next to the hotel (paid) are definitely a plus. We managed to stay overnight at a promotional price, so the value for money was definitely a plus. The building itself has been restored and well-maintained from the outside.
neg_text: Unfortunately, we didn't like the room, we didn't feel comfortable there and we wouldn't recommend it to anyone. It was obvious that his glory days were behind him. The bathroom in particular, although relatively clean, is very worn and needs renovation. The bedspreads were torn and stained in places. After turning on the blinds with the remote control, we were covered in a cloud of dust; it seemed no one had been there for a long time. At the promotional price, it's worth it for one night, but I definitely wouldn't stay longer.

title: A disappointment, but in a good location
pos_text: ...a truly excellent location. Hassle-free luggage storage before and after your stay (generally the staff was rather nice and helpful). The breakfasts smelled nice but we didn't buy them so it's hard to say for sure. It's nice that there's a patio behind the hotel that you can use
neg_text:
The patio is clearly not monitored (or the curfew is not enforced) because throughout our stay we were disturbed by loud conversations and music until very late hours.
For information, the review is for a double room with a shared bed.
To start with we were given a card and a key. Strange and not very convenient, the card only turns on the electricity in the room, the key only allows you to enter through the door.
Upon check-in, there was no sign of cleaning in the room, except for the bed being made. There was hair everywhere (definitely not ours), dust, some lint, etc. The black grout in the bathroom also suggests that this was not a one-time problem. Additionally, room service is available for an additional fee, what about longer stays?
The lack of amenities also resulted in the scarcity of soap and bath liquid (in other words, the lack of any disposable cosmetics, which are standard), just 1.5 rolls of cheap and short toilet paper.
In the bathroom, the lighting was also partially not working and there was no option to turn off the terribly loud fan. The tap in the sink only worked with a motion sensor and this was rather inconvenient and generally seems like an absurd choice in a hotel.
The bed was not very comfortable, low and terribly small. My feet were sticking out from under it and the width was more like a large one, not a small two.
They even skimped on a second desk chair or an ice tray for the freezer.
The appearance, of course, depends on taste, but we didn't like it. So unkempt, it looked like some old student dormitory.
Overall I don't recommend it, but...

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The location is good, about 5 minutes to the center.
neg_text:
We did not receive the room booked as described, but instead a large, accessible room for people with disabilities. Everything is outdated and not furnished in a modern style.

The breakfast was okay, but it was the same thing every day or every other day, which was a shame.
Mattresses very soft.
Just FYI, no German TV, but that didn't bother us.
In conclusion, it's okay for sleeping for one or two nights.

title: We stayed here for 9 days…. 9 days to long. Fortunately the beautiful City made up for the disappointing accommodation
pos_text: Location was good
neg_text: When booking the hotel, the room selected was nothing like the room we were given. Very disappointing! Dated bathroom. Serviced after a 5 day stay. I would compare our room to a UK travel lodge.

title: Value for money approx.
pos_text: The hotel is beautiful but underfunded
neg_text:
Decor.
Mood.
Furniture.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: The two young receptionists on my first day were extremely rude and lacking in culture. Apart from that, the service, especially in the dining room, was nice and helpful.

title: Passable
pos_text: The only positive thing was the hotel's location!
neg_text: The pictures did not match the room.

title: Great location, overpriced for a dated hotel, reception staff need to be customer focused and not phone focused.
pos_text: Location
neg_text:
No coffee in the room, wasn’t allowed to check in until exactly 15:00,
No toilet facilities in reception despite a long drive to the hotel the receptionist saw both my wife and I try to use the toilet and could have offered an alternative or let us check in 20 minutes early, Instead, decided to ignore a paying customer and concentrate on doing nothing.
Breakfast didn’t look good so didn’t bother with it

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Good breakfast, close to the market.
neg_text:
Garbage at the entrance to the parking lot and rats running around, no rodent control grids,
On the ground floor it is not possible to open the window and air the room.

title: Passable

neg_text:
the room doesn't look like in the pictures at all, we took king room with a double bed and we got 2 single beds connected to eachother.
Water in the shower was very low pressure which made it extremely hard to wash hair the towels were worn out and smelly, the fridge was smelly, the tv didn't work.

title: Never seen anything like it!
pos_text: The location
neg_text: I've been working in a hotel for 40 years and traveling at least 2-3 times a year for 45 years, and I've never stayed in a so-called 3-star hotel without daily cleaning, and that's especially true with a rate of €155.00 per night, room only, no breakfast! When I inquire at reception, they tell me that if I request the service it is to be paid separately, which is not specified in any way on the hotel's page! I hope that hotel service standards in Poland are not like this!

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Great breakfast
neg_text: Odor in the room.

title: Passable
pos_text: Arrangement
neg_text: It was poorly cleaned.

title: Deception
pos_text: It’s very close to rynek
neg_text: The room was untidied , food on the fridge and dirty in general

title: Don't go to this hotel in winter 🥶
pos_text:
The hotel's location is close to the center, a few minutes' walk away.
Attractive building from the outside
neg_text:
Internally The hotel is cold, it's reminiscent of an administrative building, like a hospital. Cold, therefore, in a figurative sense, but especially, as far as we are concerned, in a literal sense… We arrived around 10 pm and at the time we didn't realize it, but our room was NOT HEATED… even though the outside temperatures were below freezing…. Incredible but true… I couldn't take a shower. I found the radiators were at 0 so I turned the radiators on and the air conditioning on heating mode. My wife went to bed fully dressed; she caught a cold. It was after 2.5 hours that it warmed up (the ceilings are very high, the windows too and it was unbearable, I was only able to take a shower around 3 am). I went to point it out afterwards at reception and I had to insist that the person take note and pass on my complaint.
The next day I went to reception to see the manager but they just gave me a form to fill out with the manager's email address…. I complained to reception, however, and all they could think to say was that she slept with the window open at night…. As if I cared to know… I am very disappointed and do not recommend this hotel, except in spring and summer.
Nothing there is warm, literally or figuratively.

title: Passable
neg_text: The acoustics are very poor, you can hear everything from the corridor and the rooms.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The rooms are spacious and well furnished.
neg_text:
We arrived at the hotel at 2.48pm, but were not allowed to check in because it is from 3pm so we had to wait 12 minutes, together with another couple
STRANGE, I also rent out holiday apartments and a boarding school and if people arrive earlier this is not a problem, unless the room is not ready of course

The pillows were also of such poor quality that you could not use them. I used the bedspread,

and the windows are single glazed, so you could hear every car passing by

title: Ok for the price but not what you ordered
pos_text: Location is perfect
neg_text:
Wasn’t what I ordered. Instead of nice room with double bed I got some room for disabled people with 2 not really comfortable beds, pillows ( disaster )
I’m sure they have lovely rooms there but they advertise one room and you get something far from it. Ok for one - two nights if you only sleep there for few hours.

title: I do not recommend it. For a similar price, I would prefer to be 300-500 meters further from the center
pos_text: Location.
neg_text: The photos on booking are great but they don't reflect reality. The bed was beautifully made but we were met with rolled up sheets and a terrible green bedspread. Blue pillows and a white duvet - as if they didn't match. It was good that there was extra bedding, so we took 2 pillows each to sleep well. The bed is comfortable. Comfortable sofa but dirty as if from "mucus" was a bit scary. In the bathroom, the curtain is hanging on some cable… very poor quality soap. There is no TV on the wall facing the bedroom (not a problem) but there were cables hanging there and some "modem/decoder" that we kept hitting. TV on the couch side with a few channels and cables hanging around. The remote control is hard to operate for retirees over 90. The buttons that turn on the light are impossible to grasp. LED driver - despite many attempts we don't know what it was lighting up - probably nothing. The number of textures and colors is overwhelming. We entered a very cold room. At minus 8, you could heat up the room before checking in. Ladies at the reception sitting on their phones. There were two of them and they didn't give the impression that they knew what they were doing. Breakfast…. Probably the worst meal we've ever had in a hotel. It was relatively clean, there was an iron and a board to work on - that's a plus. The lack of tea despite having a kitchenette is another of many disadvantages. I feel cheated regarding the photos on booking.com. It was my husband's birthday weekend and I felt embarrassed by the situation.

title: I do not recommend it, there are many more hotels at this price with better service and a higher standard in Wrocław.
pos_text: Position
neg_text: Room, reception staff, TV with two working channels

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The location is a plus, and there's a lot of space in the room. The decor is typical hotel, but very nice.
neg_text: Unfortunately, the bathroom stank of urine - definitely one of those stenches that has been embedded in the tiles for years and can't be gotten rid of. Above the bed, the LED from the fire alarm system was flashing brightly, making it impossible to sleep without blindfolds. The internet would turn off late in the evening and only come back on in the morning. A night lamp with a burned-out, barely lit and flickering bulb.

title: I will never go back there again
pos_text: Hot water - there was never a moment when it wasn't hot - and air conditioning
neg_text: lack of basic bathroom equipment - toilet brushes (the staff said that these are the standards!!!) and consequently - the lack of cleanliness, amenities, comfort and value for money is at the lower end.

title: Very poor
pos_text: Location, 800m from the old town and unfortunately that's the only plus...
neg_text: Bad smell in the room, clogged drains, dirty carpet (it's scary to stand there barefoot). The rooms have long been in need of renovation. Unfortunately, it looked much better in the photos...

title: Terrible stay, I do not recommend it.
pos_text: Unfortunately, nothing is happening, it's a tragedy
neg_text:
On the day of our departure, my partner and I went for breakfast at 8:30 and unfortunately it couldn't be called breakfast, just some leftovers for pigs. There was nothing to eat, there were some leftover sausages in the pressure cookers, not to mention scrambled eggs, which were missing :/ unfortunately there was nothing to eat, no bread, no cold cuts, no cheese, only some old hard tomatoes left because no one wanted to eat from what we noticed.
Unfortunately, there was nowhere to sit either, which was a disaster.
I do not recommend and will never visit this hotel again.

title: Nice hotel. The comfort of using rooms can be improved.
pos_text: The hotel is located in a good spot, close to the attractions of Wrocław.
neg_text: Poorly soundproofed rooms. We were given a room different from the one in the picture. Access to the bed is only from one side.

title: Passable
pos_text: Location
neg_text: The room was definitely different from the one in the photo. The furnishings are worn out and the bed is uncomfortable. The ventilation in the bathroom is very noisy. Overall depressing.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Friendly reception girl, spacious room, location right next to the Old Town!
neg_text:
When you open the door, there's a stain on the floor, it looks like the previous guests spilled something and the maid hadn't washed the floor, the toilet is spacious, but the toilet bowl wasn't really clean, the electric kettle is leaking water somewhere underneath!
If I hadn't been so tired, I would have asked for another number!
I reserved a parking space, it took a very long time to get a response about the parking space reservation... The parking space is not really well thought out, you can't get in until you've checked in! On the one hand, it's understandable, on the other hand...where to leave the car while you're checking in?

It's ok for a one night stopover!

title: Overall satisfactory for a few nights.
pos_text: The location was ideal and close to the city. The staff were courteous and helpful.
neg_text: The room was very sparsely furnished and partly unclean. Furthermore, the light from the smoke detector was so bright at night in the darkness that it illuminated the entire room.

title: Tolerably.

neg_text: The room is significantly different from the photos on booking.com.

title: Passable

neg_text: The room did not match the description and photos when ordering, the bed for two was narrower. Upon arrival, the temperature in the room was 15 degrees (in February), the light from the faulty ceiling fire alarm was constantly flashing, water from the shower stall was spilling all over the bathroom - poor shower stall gradient, impractically placed light switches and blinds by the bed behind the pillow, non-functional room card. The hotel manager promised to fix/at least shade the flashing light, but did nothing.

title: Passable
pos_text: Overall, the price is adequate to the appearance of the room
neg_text: We don't know if it was like this in every hotel room, but it's a strange feeling when you get a disabled room without marking it or writing about the request for such a room. And last night there was a bug in the bathroom.

title: Pleasant

neg_text: The room we booked did not match the photos in the offer. It looked completely different than in the photos.

title: It's okay as a means to an end if you need a bed in a good location, but definitely not a hotel for an extended stay. or

neg_text: The accommodation does not match the photos on Booking at all. It appears to be a converted hospital where most of the furniture has remained as is, with only a double bed being added. The decor was somewhat uninspired and functional, but the worst thing was the constant chlorine/disinfectant smell, reminiscent of a hospital. When asked why false images were being used in the advertising, there was no satisfactory answer and no cancellation or refund.

neg_text: The room, although large and beautiful, was not completely clean. SUPER hard mattress and horrible horrible pillow.

I even double checked whether it was the room I booked, and I booked not the cheapest one. Thanks a girl at the reception for being so helpful and even changing the room. But the upgrading didn’t really help. I got the answer that photos on booking are ‘just for example’.

title: Surprised but not disappointed.
pos_text: Very good location, right next to the old market square. The air conditioning in the room was useful because it was very warm on the 5th floor. Comfortable bed. Nice ladies at the reception.
neg_text: We didn't understand that the photos on booking were only illustrative and we were surprised that the room looked completely different from the one in the photos. Rather, it is of a lower standard and adapted to the needs of disabled people - pipes in the bathroom, bed below the knees, low cabinets and plenty of space.

title: Weak

title: Passable

neg_text: A very delegation-oriented atmosphere. Only small towels available in the room.

title: Meh

Paid for a king deluxe room got placed in a accessible room with a bed that was basically the same level as the floor and me and my husband were provided with two single douvets. Old fashioned hotel.

title: Passable

neg_text: The soundproofing of the rooms was very poor and the shower was very bad.

title: Chosen basen on misleading information

neg_text: The room, we had king room which had very small double bed, very uncomfortable. The room had airco but it was not working. It was on the 5th floor so it was very hot inside. When we opened a window it was very noisy from the street. Difficult to sleep at all!

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The position. Practically in the center. Cleaning.
neg_text: I had to park the motorbike on the street. If it says there is parking on booking, there must be. Serious

title: Passable
pos_text:
The breakfast was excellent.
Close to the historic city.
Parking is excellent.
Large room.
Large shower with toilet.
Large desk.
Small but good refrigerator.
neg_text:
The service was bad.
The air conditioning broke down on the first day of our stay. The temperature in the room rose to 26°C. This was much too warm. After reporting to reception, nothing happened. The third report was that the air conditioning was broken and that a new one had to be purchased. Fortunately, the window could be opened, so the temperature could be lowered. And with the kettle, limited regulation was possible.
There is 1 chair in the room. When asked whether a second seat could be obtained, the answer was negative. That's quite inconvenient, because the bed is too low to sit on.
The shower hose was broken. This must have been the case for a while, but apparently everyone thinks it is normal. This was reported, but not yet resolved when we left.
The bed is too low. You can't stand up properly. If it is 10 cm higher, it is possible. The bed is against a wall on two sides. So one person has to get out of bed from the foot end. This is extremely inconvenient, especially because it is too low. The bed is not for seniors.
There was a German company, which was very noisy. One night they stayed in the patio talking until half past seven in the morning.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The old town was easily accessible by bus and train.
neg_text:
The staff were somewhat unprofessional and could have been friendlier. The hairdryer didn't work properly and kept switching off during use. After several inquiries, we received a very old but functioning hairdryer as a replacement.

The beds were somewhat uncomfortable and the sheet for the mattress cover kept slipping.

title: Poor
pos_text: Location: spa area
neg_text: The parking fee of PLN 80 per day was a horror for the porter who scolded me right away for taking too long to collect the parking ticket. There are barely 2 coffee capsules in the room.Room delete like a little kitten noise from the bus depot opposite.

title: Passable
neg_text: High price, you had to pay extra for parking.

neg_text: The rooms were far too warm, and the air con wasn’t much good .

title: Passable

title: Well, this time I made a mistake and was fooled by the name of the hotel chain and 5* - next time I will read our reviews carefully.
pos_text: Quiet place, quiet rooms on the 5th floor. Floor, comfortable bed, but that's where it ends, this hotel might have been 5 stars a few years ago, there's not even a minibar in the room, the payment in advance also surprised me...
neg_text: When asked about parking, it is not possible to reserve in advance, the breakfast is barely average and a big disappointment, there is no main light in the room, in the evening you have to light a lamp by the bed, otherwise old equipment, just an ordinary 3* hotel

title: Pleasant

neg_text: Breakfast could be improved, room windows very dirty, room partly dusty.

title: The hotel is okay, but personally I would never give it 5 stars.

neg_text: The hotel is showing its age and is suitable for renovation.

neg_text: One of the two saunas was damaged (no door)

neg_text: Insufficient bathroom equipment for a 5-star hotel

title: Passable
neg_text: Our room was changed three times due to a booking error: we were given one bed instead of two separate beds. After changing the room, only one bed was ready, so another change. 90s decor, old furniture. There is no panoramic view because the windows are very dirty. Breakfast was poor for a Radisson 5*

title: A hotel with that name and 5 stars should be ashamed, because the service should really be up to par and everything should be perfect.
pos_text: The location
neg_text:
What I found most disappointing was the lack of parking for guests in the hotel's own underground garage. The reason given was that strangers could also park there, so I had to find a parking space in the public parking lots.
As for the room, it smelled very strongly of disinfectant, which we reported to reception shortly after our arrival. Therefore, we had to leave the window tilted open for several hours… Thank God we were on the road, otherwise we would definitely have gotten headaches.
Breakfast was bad, because shortly before 10 a.m. no more baked goods were replenished, so only leftovers were available. The hot plates with the food were lukewarm, so the scrambled eggs and all the other dishes were not kept warm enough. Cold platters with cheese and sausage were, well… not very convincing. There was no salmon left, or perhaps there never was, and this was already shortly before 10 a.m.…

title: Nothing out of the ordinary.
pos_text: Comfortable bed.
neg_text: The toilet seat was smeared with feces, the sink was chipped, the shower was small and leaked, and the hairdryer in the bathroom didn't work. Poor location, it's better to choose something closer to the center.

title: Passable
pos_text: Breakfast, location if someone is traveling with a dog, a great place, there is a place to go for a walk with the dog.
neg_text:
Bathroom cleanliness (hair in the bathtub, dirty glasses)
It's not worth saving on the quality of coffee at breakfast, good coffee doesn't cost much :)

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Large well-equipped room.
neg_text: The gentleman at the reception definitely needs training. Far from 4-star service (behavior, more customer focus, no smiles, etc.) When checking in, I asked for a room facing the street and the gentleman said that's what he would give me. The room was not on the street side. After several stays at this hotel, I have the impression that the hotel was built on a grand scale, but its maintenance and staff are of a rather low level of management.

title: Average, we were satisfied in the past
pos_text: Room, quietness, hotel appearance
neg_text:
The lady at the reception desk, seeing us, made a bored face, went to the computer and didn't say a word. The body language was downright off-putting. No greeting, no invitation, no smile, no signal that we can start a conversation.
She charged us for parking as if it was behind a barrier, I don't know if it was right or wrong, in the past we parked in front of the barrier completely free of charge, with the consent and knowledge of the staff.

title: livable
pos_text: good breakfast, sufficient variety of products, aromatic coffee to start the day, a big plus, tasty, various fruits, good juices, cheeses. in the main hall of the hotel there is an interesting exhibition of Old mobiles.
neg_text: Unfortunately, for a 4-star hotel, there are no basic amenities - no hotel slippers, even though they were stated in the room specifications. Intervention at the reception desk twice. Unfortunately - for allergy-friendly rooms (that's what the specifications say) why is there dog hair on the armchair, carpet and curtains? There should be separate rooms for guests with pets. The vacuum cleaner cannot remove hair stuck in the carpets. The smell that greets guests in the main hall is very aggressive, very irritating, like cheap floor cleaner that has been over-poured. Bathroom - inspection by the cleaning staff supervisor is necessary - the shower cubicle is uncleaned, the grout, tiles, and the faucet underneath have 3-week-old residue. This is not for a 4-star hotel, but for a 1-star one. It is unacceptable for a guest to enter the large room with a buffet breakfast. This is what happened on October 20, 2024. The lady with the dog leaned over every dish displayed on the displays/counters, the dog was snorting "adorably," the lady touched every shared cutlery used by subsequent guests to serve food, then she put her hands under the dog's butt. This is the spread of zoonotic diseases and a lack of respect for hygiene. The dog then sat at the table on two velvet chairs, leaving behind saliva and other excrement. Later, someone in a black suit will sit comfortably on it.

title: The hotel is adequate but because of location doubt I would stay here again.
pos_text: The room was nice and big the bed was very comfortable. Catching the tram to downtown was easy except it’s a half mile from the hotel.
neg_text: The water pressure in the shower was not good but okay I guess. We didn’t have breakfast included in our rate and when we asked how much $$ for breakfast we were shocked at the price per person. Way overpriced.

title: Passable
pos_text: Very clean, nice decor, location despite being off the beaten track, very good transport connections to the center of Wrocław, even at night.
neg_text: We were supposed to get a room with one large bed, but instead we got two separate beds pushed together and they were falling apart. In one of the beds there was a sunken mattress (kind of like a hole) where you could fall into it. With the window open it is quite noisy at night due to the proximity of the road. The towels are one soft and thick, the other one is worn and hard. The bathroom was terribly stuffy and the shower was squeaking a lot (as if it was coming from pipes).

title: Pleasant
neg_text:
I didn't like that there were two beds

separate and not in pairs

title: Pleasant
neg_text: The shower gel container was empty. No containers for toothbrushes, no other cosmetic items such as cotton pads, cotton swabs, etc. Behind the kettle was a huge, dried stain of spilled coffee. For 5 stars, poor.

VERY HIGH PRICE FOR A PARKING SPACE FOR A MOTORCYCLE FOR WHICH YOU PAY HALF OR NOT AT ALL BECAUSE IT CAN BE PARKED IN A SPACE THAT CANNOT BE USED FOR PARKING CARS. I PAID PLN 110

title: We saw the starts in the sky but not in the hotel
pos_text: Perfect location near the center, easy underground parking (110 plz), a nice building and a cozy courtyard, dogs accepted (100 plz), very clean.
neg_text:
We booked superior double room with a large bed. The room had two separate beds covered with one sheet. It was no problem to change the room. However the bed in the next room “the large” bed was very small, 150 cm wide. Since we arrived late we did not want to bother to ask for a third room.
The second bad surprise was an extremely uncomfortably hard mattress.
Air conditioning started ventilate very loud at 4 AM, we did not succeed to switch it off.
The room had a bathtub with a glass wall instead of real shower which was presented in the photos for a superior room. There was only instant coffee facilities in the room.
The breakfast was basic, missing nicer presentation and better quality products (fresh orange juice, white cheese, better quality croissants or tomatos). Tables were not set up with tableware. No indication that it was possible to choose a la carte an omelet or maybe other dishes.
Suggestion: if at the reception it would be explained about the facilities (e.g. twin beds/king bed, shower or bath etc) in the room being attributed, everyone would avoid frustration.

title: Bad
pos_text: Large room and clean. The restaurant has very good food and the wait is not long. Reception always on call. Very large selection on the breakfast menu.
neg_text: On Saturday, from 6 a.m., the construction crew was carrying out renovations and there was no sleep. It was terrible because it was very loud and when you come for a weekend to relax, you expect it to be like that in a 5-star hotel, but it was exactly the opposite. After reporting the situation at the reception, I received a reply that it was under renovation and they wouldn't do anything about it...

Breakfasts were not covered by sufficient capacity and the staff "didn't have time" to set the tables.
The room cleaning was inadequate, they didn't notice a dirty coffee mug during the entire stay, which surprised me quite a bit for a hotel of this level.
Overall, the hotel corresponds to 3 stars, apparently it is just "riding" on the previous quality and name of the hotel group.

title: Potentially a great hotel, that appears to be badly managed.
pos_text: Location, parking and the room itself were nice
neg_text: The shower temperature gauge was broken, meaning there was only ice cold water at first. After we called the reception, the technician came quickly - he was friendly and competent, but it was clear that the bathroom infrastructure was old and seemed underinvested. He could at least get the water a bit warmer, but you could not adjust the temperature further otherwise it would be boiling hot or ice cold again. Then at breakfast, the rather young and seemingly friendly personnel was running around clueless. They didn't see when things were running empty at the buffet. Our pepper mill was broken, it was impossible to get someone's attention - in the end we took it from another table. Also other guests were joking about the personnel's ability to ignore everyone in the breakfast room. The breakfast itself was okay, but not the quality and choice you might expect. At reception, personnel was okay but not as professional as you could expect - English was not optimal and they seemed largely unexperienced.

title: A good base for many attractions, but the hotel itself did not impress me.

neg_text:
Average breakfasts during which the tables were cleaned so rarely that there was nowhere to sit (apart from the tables for club members).
Average rooms.

We probably won't return to this facility.

neg_text:
1. They conducted a party at the restaurant till 2a.m. so we cannot sleep. I have called a few times to the reception because of noise but I was told “we cannot do anything”. This is just unacceptable for 5* hotel: both party itself and the answer.
I definitely will not recommend this hotel for business trips.
2. Bed sheets and curtain were dirty(with some spots)
3. They did not see my prepaid booking until I arrived because of technical issues. But this was resolved by receptionist.

Breakfasts at Radisson used to be more varied, now they are 5/10. There were no bathing supplies in the bathroom, the room was only moderately prepared.

title: Rooms are great, one staff member needs to be replaced. The rest of the top

title: I do not recommend this hotel, as its level clearly does not correspond to 5 stars.
pos_text: The hotel has a great location, just a 10-12 minute walk to the Old Town Market Square.
neg_text:
We visited this hotel for Christmas, but unfortunately, our stay there ruined the holiday.

On the day of our arrival, the hotel’s kitchen stopped working at 6:00 PM, so we couldn’t order dinner.
This seemed strange, as the hotel had been constantly sending me spam emails about their Christmas menu.

There was no drinking water in the room; instead, there was an empty bottle on the table. The minibar was also empty.
When we raised this issue, the hotel staff rudely told us that we should fetch water ourselves from a cooler on the floor.
As for the empty minibar, they claimed that "this is the hotel’s policy."

The room was cold at night, even though we set the heater to maximum.
The bathroom and toilet had no heating at all, so we had to leave the door ajar to ensure a comfortable temperature by morning.

Breakfasts were mediocre and repetitive.
The carpet in the elevator was dirty, and they didn’t clean it during our entire stay.

Finally, the cherry on top: when we were checking out, the hotel attempted to charge us double for breakfasts.
So, be careful – this might be their usual practice.

title: Disappointing.
pos_text: The location was perfect, close to the old square, but in a quiet area.
neg_text:
The room smelled bad and damp, as if someone had cleaned the carpet of vomit but didn't get all the smell away.
The room was very worn out, and the beds are rock hard and made up with rubber sheets etc. So the body can't breathe, and you lie there and are wet and clammy all night.👎
The breakfast has a nice selection, but the rolls are not baked and only thawed!!!!! And all cold cuts, etc. are not refrigerated, so it's a pure bacteria bomb.

title: Not recommended for Tesla owners, although a destination charger is available!
pos_text: The hotel's location in the city center is great. Lots of restaurants nearby, but still quiet.
neg_text:
The Tesla Destination Charger was our number one selection criterion!
We were charged 110 zloty per day for parking a Tesla (80 zloty per day for a regular parking space), even though we were informed upon arrival that the charging station was broken. We were actually able to charge it briefly for about an hour once in three days. That was about 13 kWh! It is not right to charge for the full e-parking space for three days. When I checked out, they didn't want to understand me anymore!!! That was unnecessary!
Incidentally, most petrol vehicles were directed to the parking lot with charging ports.
By the way, the parking garage is very flat. Unfortunately, my trunk lid touched the ceiling and now has scratches.

Even though we chose a "standard plus" room. Old TV with limited channel selection. The cleanliness left much to be desired. The air conditioning from the restaurant next door made it impossible to sleep at night. Greenwashing - a lot of emphasis on big words about ecology - a bit too much, because this ecology is not really visible in the hotel. We understand that breakfasts are subject to a fee, but espresso coffee and water can be provided. It's nothing special, but it makes a big difference.

The hotel treats you as someone demanding instead of a customer and tells you to go to the reception (they don't answer the phone)and ask for cleaning and changing towels. Drama. As a result, he doesn't fulfill the request anyway and your room is a mess, there is no change of towels, liquids, or water. Terribly stupid SAVINGS. I complained about it at the reception desk, asking for a discount, but I was ignored and ignored.
3. Dark room, energy-saving lighting.

The plug is damaged, and the temperature cannot be adjusted with the thermostat 5. The hotel does not issue a receipt, so you have to ask several times.
6. Breakfast included in the price of PLN 75/person, so for a family PLN 215 or 300. Haha.

ignoring the customer. Rude, pompous.

At around 400 PLN per night I think you can find better options.

I reported this to booking last 2nd of jan but until the booking has no reply about this. It was just annoying that the staffs during the breakfast and the lady in the reception kept on saying that I just reserved 2persons even i had the screen shots that we are three persons.
