neg_text: The whole room reeked of cigarettes, probably from the bathroom ventilation. This made all our clothes smell like cigarettes. We are non-smokers and this strongly discouraged us from staying at this hotel. Additionally, the room itself is showing its age and this is visible in many of the decor elements. You also get the feeling that the material elements are not very clean. Additionally, we stayed overnight on New Year's Eve and loud music could be heard all night long - I don't know if it was from the hotel itself or if the hotel is connected to residential buildings and that's where the noise came from.

title: Disappointing

neg_text: The hotel is far from 4 stars, with mold in the shower, a cracked sink, a broken toilet brush, a clogged sink, bedside lamps with peeling paint, and the bathroom definitely needs renovation.

neg_text: The hotel is old, the furniture is torn, the condition of the bathroom is visible in the photo, it is not worth the money. Additionally, they write that they have parking included in the price, but take into account that you have to pay an additional PLN 90 per day, it's not fair.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The location is excellent

title: I don't know where these 4 stars come from
pos_text: Very comfortable beds, tasty breakfast (although small selection). The location has its pros and cons, we stayed midweek so it wasn't as noisy as it probably is on weekends. Very friendly and helpful staff. Adorable pillow menu - big plus!
neg_text:
It's hard to objectively call the hotel a four-star hotel - maybe it once met the requirements, today it's an average three-star hotel. The decor in the photos looks great, but in reality the hotel is worn, torn and cracked. As a host, I would be ashamed to provide guests with a room where the shower didn't work - it was impossible to set the shower handle at the right level above the head because it kept sliding down - the mechanism is probably x years old. The door to the room had a large gap between the frame, which intensified the audibility of sounds from the corridor. In the room we found a used capsule in the coffee machine, dirty water with limescale in the kettle, and dirty spoons, which effectively discouraged us from using this equipment. No iron in the room. The hair dryer is a relic of old swimming pools, it only dries short hair (men's), drying women's hair would probably take 3 days. The air conditioning was unbearably loud, it would have been impossible to sleep with it on - we decided to open the windows instead, which would probably have been impossible on the weekend due to the noise from the courtyard (the restaurant below the windows). The elevator smelled like carpet that had never been washed.
The hotel is simply begging for renovation.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location
neg_text:
You had to pay for cleaning separately.
They charged us for breakfast that they said wasn't included in the price, even though the reservation was for a double room with breakfast included.

title: Disappointment with the sales manager
pos_text: For the overall hotel, I would give 10 because we have been to the hotel many times, so as a whole, i.e. The rooms, food, comfort, reception service and location are all great and we will definitely come back, however, this time we were very disappointed with the approach of the sales manager and her ignorance of the problem, despite the willingness to help from other employees, whether from the reception, marketing or sales department. We have a lesson for the future.
neg_text: We couldn't come, we weren't there, and due to a problem with the booking application, we couldn't confirm the cancellation of the reservation, and even though it was free and the booking service wanted to help, the hotel didn't mark the cancellation and, even though we weren't at the hotel, they charged us for the stay. We wanted to explain this and despite our attempts and the attempts of the booking service, the hotel (or more precisely the sales manager, as the rest of the staff wanted to help) she was unwilling to cooperate, although we know she could have refunded us for a stay we didn't attend, especially since, from what we were told, the hotel's room occupancy on those dates was minimal, so we weren't blocking anyone out, and our room was one of many the same ones throughout the property.

title: Very poor
pos_text: In one sentence, the stay at this hotel is saved by the staff, who are very polite. And unfortunately nothing more.
neg_text:
Omnipresent dirt!!! The curtains in the room are sticky with dust. The hotel corridors are dark with dirty and dusty carpeting. Lack of room cleaning during the stay is acceptable in a two- or three-star hotel, but the Scandic hotel has four of them. It seems that these stars should be verified. When you touch anything in the hotel, you feel like your hands are covered in all kinds of bacteria and viruses.
The underground car park is not secured, which is indicated not by the staff but by the inscription on the parking ticket.
Too small a restaurant for the number of hotel rooms. To eat breakfast during a long weekend, you have to wait in line for a free table. And once you're inside, you discover there are no plates, cups, knives or spoons. The staff tried to make up for it with a good face and an apologetic smile, but such things have no right to happen in a four-star hotel.

title: The staff is dishonest. They charged extra.
neg_text: The staff is dishonest. They charged extra.

From the outside, the windowsill would look as if no one had ever washed it. The cabinets are worn out on the outside and a disaster on the inside. You could stick to the furniture. For almost 1000 PLN for two nights I expected something much better!

Tried to get some currency exchanged for smaller denominations but the front desk wouldnt do it. Oir room phone wasnt working properly so the sent up a technition but he couldnt fix it and we never saw him again. All in all, I wouldnt reccomend the Scandic Hotel.

Even in the evening when we returned to the hotel, there was still drilling and hammering going on not far from our room.

title: Not up to 4-star standards.
pos_text: The location was really good. Close to the city center, restaurants and public transport.
neg_text:
This is not a 4-star hotel. I have stayed at Scandic hotels in several countries, and the standard here was significantly lower than what I am used to. The room was very dusty. My partner is allergic to dust, and he had a very bad reaction whenever we spent more than two hours in the room. When we moved things around (such as taking the cover off the bed or hanging a towel to dry), you could clearly see dust in the air. The room also had a fitted carpet, which was not very clean either.

We stayed from Thursday to Sunday and were informed that there would be no cleaning or towel changes unless we specifically requested it. This is not what I would expect from a 4-star hotel.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: We liked the hotel's location, so this was not our first stay at this facility. However, there is a huge gap between standard and standard plus rooms. We didn't expect it, because in other hotels, the difference is maybe only in the size of the room, coffee machine, etc. And here, as if they were two different hotels. We had previously stayed in a standard plus room and it was nice and modern. This time we chose a standard room and it was as if we had gone back in time 20-30 years. Fungus on the grout around the bathtub, weak water pressure in the shower, wallpaper in the room old, dirty, and sticking out. Unvacuumed floor.
neg_text: Mold in the bathroom, old, dirty, peeling wallpaper. Not very clean.

title: Charm of a youth hostel/Hostel
pos_text: The central location and the parking facilities.
neg_text: The room furnishings are very outdated. The beds are unusually narrow and very low, so they might be uncomfortable for older visitors. The rooms do not have conventional showers, but only bathtubs equipped with a shower screen. The hotel staff, hotel guests and service are more reminiscent of a youth hostel/hostel than a good hotel from the SCANDIC group. A considerable amount of work is needed to achieve a good minimum standard. My next stay in Wrocław will definitely be in a more "modern" hotel.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Good breakfast, fresh juices.
neg_text: No heating in the room

title: If the hotel improves, we will come back.
pos_text: The location is very good.
neg_text: The beds were very bad; the mattresses shifted with every movement. The bathroom was dirty and the fixtures were in need of renovation.

title: When you check in, check everything right away. We were tired and didn’t bother to figure it out, which we regretted.
neg_text: The floors looked like they hadn't been washed, and there was trash under the bed. There is a lot of mold along the seams in the bathroom, the chairs are stained, and there are stains on the walls. The towels are stained. The dust above the bed was not wiped. The bed was listed as Queen size, but in fact it was two small ones put together, it was very uncomfortable to sleep.

title: Disappointing
neg_text: Previously, I booked a superior room twice. This time the room was smaller. The hotel has kept the name of the rooms but made them smaller. Renovating rooms but keeping old bathrooms doesn't fit the new look. Super smart TV but only two German channels (RTL and N-tv)

title: I rate the stay at 4 because of the breakfast. this would be lower.
pos_text: Breakfast ok.
neg_text: TV. Not all stations are available. They were all supposed to be stationary. Decor in some rooms from the 90s.

title: We will not return.
pos_text: The location is good.
neg_text:
There was no flow in the bathtub.
There was no soap for showering, they forgot to refill.
An outdated hotel and not at all 5 star level.

title: Very poor
pos_text: The location is next to a park.
neg_text:
How does this place have five stars?!

We had an empty shower gel bottle, a big spill on the shelf and a broken phone so could not call reception. The room itself feels worn out, like an old corporate hotel that was never updated. The fake wood has lots of scrapes and worn bits and the bathroom feels incredibly dated. Finally, like all hotels that claim to be environmentally friendly only when it saves them money, they no longer provide bottles of water, but rather a fountain you share with the whole floor.

This was an incredibly disappointing stay. The last time I stay at a Radisson for sure.

title: not very successful
pos_text: location
neg_text:
Room 527 looks like a concrete shelter, it is cold, dark, the air conditioning is too loud, the lighting is too dim. The noise from the restaurant located on the roof is disturbing.
The bar has overpriced seats, uncomfortable seats, and reverberation (terrible acoustics). being the opposite of the club atmosphere.

title: Once and never again!
pos_text:
Beautiful spa area
Pleasant atmosphere
City center location
Suitable as a party location, but not as a hotel for sleeping.
neg_text:
5 stars ???
No soundproofing, no chance of sleeping.
Unique clairaudience.
Avoid the 5th floor when the weather is nice, as the disco area is on the 6th floor.
Moving tables and chairs seems to be a popular sport.
Noise level from compressors on the roof unbearable.
Thank God the room could be swapped.

title: Discomfort
pos_text: The location is undoubtedly an advantage
neg_text: Negative aspects - reception staff poorly oriented and poorly trained. One elevator is undoubtedly a big minus, long waiting times for the elevator, e.g. After using the pool, the elevator to level -1 arrives full and you have to wait for another one, and another one, and so on, up to 3 or 4 elevators until you manage to get on, which is not very comfortable after the pool when you are standing in the corridor in a bathrobe and freezing. The lighting in the room is very dim, of course suitable for a cozy evening but poor when using the room. The curtains hang on metal cords, which makes them very difficult to move; even the staff have trouble moving them. The bathroom has very poor lighting and there is no way to cover the bathroom windows, there are no blinds, curtains, or anything else, which makes it quite difficult to use the bathroom after dark when there is a tenement house with windows opposite, and it is uncomfortable when using the toilet or taking a shower.

title: Poor room was very dark and the glass screen for bathroom area is dangerous
pos_text: Breakfast was poor not enough space around the buffet area
neg_text:
Service area around breakfast buffet was crammed
Pool area in spa not supervised children treating spa like a play area

title: I recommend it to anyone who wants to spend a weekend
pos_text: Great location, very friendly staff, breakfast OK!
neg_text: beds too short

title: Too expensive, too weak.
neg_text: Value for money. The price is definitely overstated. Breakfast is completely not worth the price of 120 PLN/person. Not very cozy, outdated interior. The bathroom is like a tomb with a mirror that is too small.

title: Sleepless in Wrocław
pos_text: Great rooftop terrace, nice restaurant, good design
neg_text: The air conditioning didn't work; the room was much too warm. We had to change rooms several times; it was too noisy outside because of humming containers under the windows, and the room was extremely noisy. Overall, we unfortunately hardly got any sleep.

title: Unfortunately, nothing out of the ordinary. Not worth 5 stars.
neg_text: The water in the sink did not drain completely. Our two complaints resulted in NOTHING. None of the employees contacted knew anything about the complaint. There was no apology either. The room was dark and not comfortable.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Overall I like this hotel: good location, interesting interior design.
neg_text:
Had reservations few times the rooms in reality are smaller and darker than on photos, which makes me feel frustrated.
This reservation it was heard very well everything happening on kitchen like in room, I think it is because of air conditioning.

title: Passable
pos_text: The location is a huge plus and this is probably the main reason for the price, but it is not 5 stars. We've stayed in many five-star hotels and this one is nothing like that. Poor and very poor room decor, only basic toiletries in the bathroom, no iron. This is not what we expected from a 5-star hotel.
neg_text: Nice staff. Location

title: Location is good, but the hotel is not 5 stars in my opinion.
pos_text: The location
neg_text:
For a 5-star hotel, the room was very spartan!
There were no coffee or tea facilities, the bathroom is large but very illogically laid out, the lighting was also mediocre, and the bed was much too low and uncomfortable.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: waiters, very amateur behavior and service for a 5* hotel

title: We simply won't come back.
pos_text: We returned to the hotel because of the location, proximity to the guarded parking lot, the hotel design and the great breakfast and good service so far. remaining location and design of the hotel.
neg_text: Being in the hotel again, we were given a room that was worn out by time, we were treated as 1 person - 1 bathrobe, 1 set of towels, 1 slippers - we returned late so we gave up calling the room service. The last straw was the bathtub, which I gave up on in the morning when I remembered getting into it in the evening. Even my very capable husband had trouble, and I will add that there was no handle installed next to the bathtub, which is a requirement in facilities categorized below. When I passed this comment on to the receptionist I heard "the handles do not match the design of the building's owners"w-well, my hands are at my feet. And breakfast? I know many 4-star hotels of a much higher standard.

title: This hotel does not deserve 5*.
pos_text: I liked the building and the historic stairs inside. Great location.
neg_text: When I first wake up, the room is filled with battered furniture and a stained armchair. There is mold in the bathroom shower. The flush cistern is broken. The rain shower on the ceiling is in terrible condition. The carpets in the hallway are stained.The noise is terrifying, you can hear everything from the corridor. The elevator is terribly slow, better to take the stairs. Standard breakfast. I might even say it's a poor choice. After reporting my concerns, they changed us to a room with a higher standard. It was definitely quieter and larger. However, the shower was not without mold either.

title: Not worth the noise.
pos_text: Special style. Bath and shower in the bathroom. Great roof terrace. German TV channels are available.
neg_text:
Extremely noisy hotel. We were woken up in the middle of the night by more than obscene noises from the guests next door. You really hear everything. Whether it's the clearing of dishes or the groans of other guests. It almost gave the impression that professional women were allowed to come and go as they pleased. Completely unbelievable and unpleasant.
The furnishings are intended to appear luxurious, but in many places they are slightly damaged or not functional.

title: Beautiful on the outside, needs renovation on the inside
pos_text: Location and beautiful building from the OUTSIDE
neg_text: The inside of the building needs refreshing. No sockets by the bed, no possibility of making a frosted glass in the bathroom. The curtains are difficult to open and close - these are probably the biggest disadvantages

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Very good SPA facility, very good location.
neg_text: Poor staff service, very poor Standard room equipment, no parking (very difficult to find a space). There are much better options in Wrocław at this price.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Style, history of the object
neg_text: I chose the hotel because of the saunas and swimming pool to relax in the evening. The pool was cold and overcrowded, the jacuzzi had the same water - very cold, the dry sauna was broken, the wet one turned off after 5 minutes and never turned back on. No relaxation

title: The hotel does not live up to its rating.
pos_text: The only positive aspect I can mention is the spa – the pool and sauna were excellent.
neg_text:
First of all, one of the water bottles in the room was already opened when we checked in. The seal on the cap clearly indicated that the bottle had been opened, and it was filled to the brim with tap water.
After we took a bath, we couldn’t drain the water because the valve was broken. I had to deal with it myself, as we didn’t want to contact the hotel on our special day – we just wanted to relax.
In the double room, there was only one set of towels, bathrobes, and slippers. We had to request an additional set for the second guest.
We stayed on the second floor, and there was some kind of corporate event happening on the first floor, which made it very difficult to rest. The music was extremely loud. After midnight, we reported it to the reception, but the music only stopped closer to 1 a.m.

It felt more like a standard 4-star hotel located in an old historic building.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: I had the pleasure of having breakfast at the hotel and I am very pleased! The choice of dishes is wide – fresh bread, delicious cheeses, cold meats, fruit, as well as warm options that were perfectly prepared. Everything is fresh and tasty, and the service is extremely polite and helpful. The pleasant atmosphere makes every morning start really well here. I definitely recommend it!
neg_text: The stay at the hotel was not entirely comfortable. The beds were too soft, which made sleeping on them uncomfortable and I woke up sore. It would definitely be nice to be able to choose firmer mattresses. Additionally, the prices in the hotel bar are definitely too high. This makes using the bar not a pleasant addition, but rather an unnecessary expense. The hotel has its advantages, but these issues need improvement.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Cold water in the jacuzzi

title: Minimalism when it comes to 5 stars. Many hotels with a penny rating do a better job.
pos_text: Architecture
neg_text: Lighting in the room (no light in the room), cold water in the jacuzzi and pool, no kettle in the room.

title: Passable
pos_text: Intimate swimming pool, salt cave, cleanliness
neg_text: Room acoustics. We spent two nights there and two nights we didn't sleep. You can hear absolutely everything from other rooms. From conversations, through flushing the toilet or taking a bath. In addition, from time to time something humming would turn on. Air conditioning or a furnace? And on the second night the cleaning ladies started vacuuming before 8 a.m., which, given the acoustics, woke us up from bed because the sound was so loud that I thought someone was vacuuming in our room... A specific window. At a height of about 150 cm with a view of the wall.

title: Needs renovation or price reduction
pos_text: Location right next to the market square. Swimming pool.
neg_text:
The hotel is old and dark, and charges high fees for everything. Definitely not worth the price.
Breakfasts are terrible, scrambled eggs made from powder. Staff is very young and poorly trained.

title: A very successful stay
pos_text: Breakfast is average, location is ok
neg_text: Appearance of the rooms

title: Dark, depressing concrete room
pos_text: Location
neg_text: The room was horrible. Bare concrete walls and ceiling. Dark floor and furnishings, little daylight and dim lighting, it was dark and depressing. It felt like an underground bunker or a crypt or something. Not the luxury 5 star romantic getaway we were looking for for our anniversary. We'd paid extra for a larger, nicer room and ended up with a room we didn't want to spend any time in! We left after the first night despite having paid in advance for two.

title: Not worth the money, plenty of other options to choose from in Wroclaw.
pos_text: Location in Wroclaw, the facade of the building, good opening hours of the spa zone.
neg_text: The property is pretending to be a luxurious location with top notch service. Unfortunately, the furnishing is tired, starting with slow single elevator, through the carpets in the hall looking as if there was a water loss, with the furniture in rooms being just worn out and the bathroom shower leaking water and barely providing any proper stream to wash. The staff while trying their best also did not satisfy, the rooftop bar could not welcome us on an evening after rainy day as they did not wipe the tables clean and we were not allowed to sit in the restaurant part to have drinks. We went to a bar right next to the hotel where we sat outside on dry chairs and enjoyed the cooler air.

title: Far too expensive, location very good. Not 5 stars. Unfortunately not recommended.
pos_text: Location was central in the city center. Friendly staff. Breakfast was okay.
neg_text: Was advertised with a swimming pool in the hotel, but has to be paid extra. Parking not included, must be paid extra. equipment Rooms are getting old. Room service bad. Rooms with minibar, but must be paid extra. Hotel more like 2 stars, not 5 stars.

title: Unacceptable stay!
neg_text: Room without opening the window, locked up like mice. There were no face towels, we asked for them to give us some. A basket in the bathroom without a lid, no basket anywhere else in the room. No refrigerator!!! and a thin table where you could only put a few things. Expensive accommodation (90€ per night) and the room unacceptable!

title: This is the worst Ibis hotel we have ever stayed at
pos_text: Unfortunately nothing!
neg_text: Where should one even begin? Ibis has apparently bought some rooms from Novotel? Unfortunately everything is bad! The breakfast was just ridiculous. Breakfast room in a corner of the hotel, separate from Novotel. The bed seemed clean, but unfortunately the skin started to itch. We booked 3 nights, but checked out after the second night. The manager was extremely helpful and tried to give us a new room. But that was out of the question for us. We received full reimbursement for parking and one night's stay.

title: Terrible experience
pos_text: Receptionist was nice
neg_text:
No heating with minus three degrees outside.
The staff sent two portable heaters to heat the room and the power went out.
As a result, I get a fever of 39.

title: Okay
pos_text: It’s a great location near the city centre
neg_text: The room wasn’t as good as expected by visiting other Ibis hotels around Poland. The toilet hasn’t been topped up (toilet paper was left half used by previous visitors etc) and it wasn’t as clean as I would want it

title: Poor
pos_text: The hotel is well-kept and the room I stayed in was spacious and, above all, very clean.
neg_text: The stay at this hotel turned out to be very disappointing. There was no electricity in the room from midnight, which made it impossible to work in the morning. I reported the fault, but despite assurances, the service did not take any action to solve the problem. I asked to speak to the shift manager and after speaking with him, I was assured that he would contact me by phone. Unfortunately, I didn't live to see it. This approach is unprofessional and disrespectful towards the guest. I expected a much better level of service and commitment with such a number of positive reviews and the price.

title: Poor
pos_text: Location, cleanliness
neg_text: Extremely dry air in the room, poorly functioning elevators or not working at all

title: Poorly
pos_text: Bed
neg_text: No fridge, no wardrobe

title: Pleasant
neg_text: High pillows, impossible to sleep on them. Short bed. Weak hairdryer. Lack of locker in the bathroom.

title: We spent three days and the room was never cleaned.
pos_text: The convenience in the center
neg_text: The neglect of the room

title: Passable
pos_text: Nice, clean room, plus a kettle with tea and coffee. Comfortable bed, clean room and bathroom.
neg_text: Low water pressure in the shower and no bottled water available in the room. Breakfast is very poor for the price. I recommend it if you need to stay overnight and travel further, but for a longer stay I would look for something better.

title: Good priced hotel near city center
pos_text: Hotel is placed in good location. Very close to city center of Wroclaw. Big parking lot with monitoring is a good thing if travel with a car. However it's additionally paid. Rooms are small but have everything necessary. Stuff is nice and friendly.
neg_text: Hotel was renovated but you can still feel that it's old. Our shower had some problems with water pressure. Also there was noticeable smell from shower drainage. Closet and cabinets in room were small and it can be difficult to store your clothes if you stay for couple of days. Also you can't open window in a room and air conditioning isn't very strong so it can be problem in warmer days. Or when you got a room with smelly bathroom.

title: Not recommended
pos_text: Location about 20 minutes from the center, well connected by tram. Outdoor parking space available at the hotel.
neg_text: The standard expected from Ibis is not maintained. The hotel appears neglected and has obvious problems; cost-cutting is taking place at every turn. Only one towel per person (so only a shower towel, not a hand towel), so little water comes out of the shower that it is difficult to wash your hair, and the water from the sink does not drain away. The windows don't open, so you have to leave the air conditioning on all the time (it worked, but it was drafty), and the beds are narrow and uncomfortable. The staff at reception were unfriendly. During our absence, the room was entered, obviously to affix a sticker, and the peel-off film was left in the bathroom. The cleaning staff knocked us out at 9 a.m. In some places at the rear of the large parking lot, the barriers are missing, so that with some effort it would be possible to drive a car out of the parking lot without authorization. Parking fee overpriced, overall poor value for money. There is currently a large construction site right next to the hotel. Although the hotel is not directly near the train station, there are sex shops on the next street. We left a day early so we wouldn't have to spend another night there. At least we were reimbursed for one day’s parking fee.

title: successful :)
pos_text: Location
neg_text: the carpet is a bit worn, the bathroom has also seen better days, both the room and the bathroom need refreshing.

title: Average
pos_text: Breakfast Okay,
neg_text: Breakfast is better at the other Ibis hotel. The location in such heat is too far from sightseeing attractions.

title: Passable
pos_text: The hotel entrance is nice and spacious and the room is large.
neg_text:
Unwelcoming reception, little attention to hospitality.
Second day there were 4 at the reception and not even a greeting!!!

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Great breakfast with a wide range of food and very friendly staff at the breakfast bar.
neg_text:
In the room, there was a disgusting smell of tobacco, like someone from the neighbours is smoking non-stop. It was impossible to be in the bathroom as well as in the room because this smell is strong and quite old. I could assume that there are some issues with ventilation.
However, after our request, the hotel staff offered another room, not in Ibis part, but in Novotel. There were no problems with the smell. The only thing is that the kettle didn't work. Overall, I think that the hotel price is not fair in terms of value/money.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Cleanliness, staff, style
neg_text: It was very cold in our room and just after our check-out (two nights) I caught a cold and our Christmas vacation in Poland turned into a huge dissapoinment

title: First impression WOW! Thereafter: NEVER AGAIN!!!
pos_text: This hotel is a true classic – in the restaurant and foyer you can feel the charm of the good old days. The wellness area also impresses with great offers. The situation couldn't be better. The design and features take some getting used to, but are very memorable. The breakfast is very plentiful; The selection is limited, but the quality of the products is good. I spent my birthday there and received a fruit basket and a birthday greeting—a very nice gesture.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, I was not satisfied with my stay. The check-in process was very reserved and unfriendly – guests should have felt much more welcome!
The first room was a disaster: The air conditioner hummed so loudly that the bed vibrated all night. Directly behind our bed was the bathroom in the next room – as soon as the tap was turned on, we were awake. I have never experienced such noise, even in old houses.
Additionally, our safe had a loose connection and was therefore unusable. We reported this to reception – they promised to take care of it, but nothing happened. The first night was terrible. We've never stayed in such a noisy hotel before—it was more like a youth hostel.
The next morning we mentioned it again and luckily we quickly got a new room. As an apology, we were invited to breakfast. The gentleman at the reception was very friendly.
The new room was much quieter, and the air conditioning and safe worked perfectly. There was even a bathtub with a whirlpool function – but it was broken, which was almost amusing. 5*??? At least we were finally able to sleep well.
The wellness area is generally nice and the staff there are very friendly. However, you shouldn’t look too closely: A strip of ledge is missing at the pool for about ten meters – presumably fallen off and never repaired. The entire area looks dirty, and there is extensive mold on the ceiling, especially above the whirlpool. The dim light conceals the whole thing a little. Be careful in the whirlpool: The stone tiles are so chipped in some places that there is a risk of injury. In addition, the loungers in the pool area are rusting badly—we ruined our bathrobes.
By the way, when we checked out, we weren't asked how we liked our stay – which is unfortunately somewhat telling.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The cleanliness and sustainability
neg_text: The windows could not be opened. There is air conditioning, however the rooms are very perfumed, making it difficult to breathe. My nose is blocked in the morning. Headaches occurred because no oxygen could be supplied.

title: The stuff knew about smell of urine when I started to complain an
pos_text: Location is good,also parking is only 60 ZL per day
neg_text: The smell of urine was SO strong in the room 228, we couldn’t sleep

title: Pleasant
neg_text: The towels are thin and worn. Curtains impossible to close - tired guide.

title: A comfortable place, but bureaucracy and customer contact could be better.
pos_text: Large comfortable bed and tasty breakfast. Nice view from the window, close to public transport.
neg_text: The sausages at breakfast were already cold around 10. There is a threshold in the bathroom that is easy to trip over. An additional downside was that a change of date was rejected a month before departure, even though the room was available on another day, without giving a reason. The owner of the facility also did not inform about the construction nearby the building.

title: Overall, I recommend staying at the hotel for 3 nights.
pos_text: Location, from the hotel to the center it is about a kilometer to walk. Air conditioning . It's nice that there's a kettle, coffee and tea in the room.
neg_text: The water pressure is very weak, it is difficult to wash long hair. No fridge.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location
neg_text: The hotel is too expensive for what it offers. Breakfast was of average quality, the room didn't look the best. Rather closer to hostel quality. I understand that Wrocław is an expensive city.

title: Passable
pos_text: Room was nice and spacious.
neg_text:
The air conditioning didn't work; only the room air was recirculated. The windows couldn't be opened, which is all the more annoying given the non-functioning air conditioning. Not very pleasant in these summer temperatures.
The water dispenser in several hallways had run out of carbonation, but still water was available.
There are no additional power outlets by the bed; one outlet is insufficient for two people.

title: Correct
pos_text: Good location and nice roof terrace
neg_text: Terribly poor service, rooftop restaurant advertises with Michelin and there is a disaster. The waiters can't remember orders. I asked for the menu three times and then they said they didn't know if there would be a table for me. How can they not know such things!!! Besides, they don't see the customer, you have to follow them and argue. The biggest minus of this hotel!!!

title: The hotel does not live up to its 5 stars
pos_text:
The fruit selection at breakfast was outstanding.
The soundproof windows saved our night's sleep.
neg_text:
Hardly any bread selection at breakfast. There was only one!!! Salad and no cream cheese for breakfast. We would have expected more in a 5-star hotel.
In our room, we had the doorknob in our hand when closing the bathroom door and the window handle when opening the window! Without words...
Behind the armchairs in the room hadn't been cleaned for a long time.
What a shame!

title: Good location, friendly service and lovely cocktails! However, a peaceful spa moment is not guaranteed!
pos_text: Friendly staff and top location! The hotel's rooftop bar was cozy and had the best drinks, so I highly recommend the rooftop bar!
neg_text: SPA. Unfortunately, the spa level did not meet expectations for a 5-star hotel. The pool was full of people and families with children and there were only a few beds next to the pool, so we really couldn't fit in there and didn't get to swim on the first day. I think the spa's standard is lowered by the fact that people outside the hotel can also buy a visit for cheap. People also didn't shower before going into the pool.

title: Nice hotel but a lot of things was not working. Overpriced
pos_text: Nice interior and spacious rooms.
neg_text: Air condition was not working. The hotel is nicely located but very far away from the city centre. The gym without any ventilation and with only four devices, one was broken. This should not be called gym.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: The maximum rating for this hotel is 5, but I give it 6. The staff was very friendly. The room was very dirty. The lack of a swimming pool in a 5-star hotel and many other downsides.

title: Never again this hotel! Please see the pictures!
neg_text:
The spa area was very small, and the cushions on the loungers were dirty!
Camera in the spa area "No-go" floor dirty with ants and larvae.

On the day of departure, there was only one trainee; she couldn't help me with my complaints because no one from management was there.
I subsequently asked management to contact me, but have not received a message to this day.

A 5-star hotel, and the price is unacceptable.

The doorman watched us carry our suitcases, he stood on the red carpet, but then he pressed the button for the elevator!

Expect a refund!

title: Passable
pos_text: The breakfast was very good and of high quality.
neg_text: We had booked the suite. The room itself is nice, but the furniture is quite worn. The cleaning ladies were not very thorough. There were spiderwebs hanging from the ceilings everywhere.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Despite a free upgrade, we were given a standard room.

title: Nice facility - especially from the outside. main part. The rear part of the hotel is located almost in a park.
pos_text: Location ok - main building on a very fast road. But behind the main building, part of the hotel is almost in a park. A very large bed. The entrance markings are a bit difficult to see - I wasn't the only one who made a mistake and drove through the first gate and the entrance is in the second one. Both gates have barriers, so you have to enter the second one. Lots of mirrors - both in the bathroom and in the room.
neg_text: The additional fee for parking - PLN 80 per day - is a lot. The bed is large but the mattress is very, very soft - not everyone will like it. Faulty shower - open (no door) the cabin causes water to splash onto the part of the bathroom outside the cabin, directly under the toilet, despite the installed threshold, and then you have to walk on water to get to the toilet, unless you waste one towel on wiping up the water. For a 5-star hotel, the breakfast could be richer. The day before I was in a 4-star hotel and the breakfast was better than the one at the Platinum. To get from the main part where the restaurant is located to the back of the hotel you have to use two elevators or an elevator and stairs - it's a bit ill-conceived.

title: Okay, but not 5 stars.
neg_text: For a 5-star hotel, the hotel room was not clean enough. Dirty curtains, bathroom not properly cleaned. Everything was rather unclean. The hotel staff were barely noticeable and seemed rather uninterested. The parking situation is tense in the evenings.

title: Passable
pos_text: The breakfast was great, but the breakfast room was too small.
neg_text:

title: It was good, for a while.
pos_text: A truly high-class hotel. The building itself is beautiful, the interior refined in every detail. SPA included in the price, including a mini gym where you can do cardio training. The room was spacious, clean, and prepared for our arrival. The hotel - as a building - met our expectations. The room was comfortable and beautifully situated, but unfortunately it was winter.
neg_text:
Service. Unfortunately, apart from the fantastic butler who let us into the premises, the reception (which should be impeccable in a hotel of this class) leaves much to be desired. During my stay, upon check-in, the amount for the night was charged to my card (the money was debited from my account) plus PLN 200 deposit. When checking out, the lady at the reception informed me that they were unable to confirm the pre-authorization from the card and that I had to pay for the accommodation again - and the original authorization would be returned by the bank at some point. Therefore, the hotel stay cost me PLN 3,000 instead of PLN 1,500. Now I am waiting for the money to be refunded by the bank and for the hotel to refund the PLN 200 deposit. I don't know what would have happened if I hadn't had the extra 1,500 PLN to re-download the transaction. I hope that the issue of refunding the overpayment will be resolved by the bank/hotel. This situation has ruined all the pleasure I had staying at your hotel. The ladies at the reception unfortunately showed a lack of professionalism and understanding of me as a customer.

Additionally, a higher standard room, which we chose for a given season (autumn) wasn't the most pleasant, the glass walls in the entire room and the heat vents weren't able to heat up our room - the nights were cold and we couldn't live without the vents roaring all night long - Unfortunately...

Unfortunately, our reservation coincided with the organization of a mass event and on Friday we were unable to use the restaurant upon arrival. The restaurant was also closed on Saturday... generally - if you are a hotel guest and in the meantime there is a mass event - you are lower in the hierarchy of needs than a random person from a corporate event. Pity.

As for the wellness, unfortunately it was one of the worst we've been to - no privacy and overall not cozy and poorly designed. Furthermore, parking is extremely expensive, even though the location in Wrocław is very far from the center. For dog walkers - an abnormally expensive fee for a dog (5 kg). Even in Switzerland, a five-star hotel in Geneva wasn't that expensive. I'm happy to pay for quality and a great wellness center, but this hotel doesn't match the price or the rating with its amenities.

Additionally, when we wanted to use the wellness area, there was no hotel staff at the reception in this part. We found a note telling us to report to the main reception if we wanted to use the SPA. Unfortunately, no one answered at the reception, so we went to the reception physically. When we arrived, the lady was clearly surprised that we had come, as the card supposedly only concerned scheduled massages and treatments. So we went back and got the towels ourselves. Overall, the organization is quite chaotic and for a five-star hotel there are quite a few shortcomings.

We phoned again & they offered to move rooms, we accepted but couldn't understand why they didn't give us that room in the first place away from the work area, when we asked we were told they had lots of rooms away from the work. Not quite the relaxing few days away we were expecting.
The room we were given was much better but not 100% finished, there was bare concrete/ reinforcing showing in the ceiling & capped off pipes for a radiator sticking through the wall, not ideal.
Staff weren't particularly helpful, customer service goes a long way & they would do well to remember that, we were given two complimentary drink vouchers as an apology but when we used them we had to pay 1zl so they weren't complimentary, not a lot of money I know but considering they were given as an apology you'd think they'd have waved it.
The whole thing just made me even more annoyed because it could have been so much better with a little forethought.
Don't stay here until the renovations are complete.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Hotel location. In the immediate vicinity of the Old Town. Very tasty and rich breakfasts. Moreover, we used the half-board option and it was a really great solution. The dishes served at the hotel reservation deserve the highest rating...large portions, beautifully presented and very tasty. Theoretically, the size of the room is a plus, but you have to take into account that I consciously chose a larger but also more expensive room.
neg_text: Well...the fitness center and spa are probably a joke. On the so-called The gym had one exercise bike - non-functional, although supposedly new, one elliptical trainer - also non-functional, one treadmill, which, to put it mildly, could be described as worn out, but its cleanliness was a disaster... dirty, sticky, the phone holder literally melted... below criticism. There was also a strength training atlas, which also dates back to very distant times. Additionally, the above equipment is set up in one room with a swimming pool, jacuzzi and sauna... so it is hot, humid and not very pleasant/comfortable.

title: I expected more from Qubus 4*..
pos_text: The location is definitely a plus, very close to everything. The room was clean, tidy, warm and quiet.
neg_text: The food, from the restaurant to the breakfast which was included in the price - was a disaster. A 4-star hotel, we assumed that the food would be first class. For dinner we ordered (on our own) ramen with duck (the duck was brown, it should have been pink, the broth tasted like water with a few drops of magic, raw carrots and mushrooms. I specifically called the waitress to witness that the carrot I bit into was crunchy because it was raw. A dish for PLN 49, completely tasteless. The żurek soup tasted and smelled like mushroom soup, and the waitress confirmed that she could smell the mushrooms :) Breakfast? - dried hams, hard cheeses, scrambled eggs made from powder (100%). Even the tea had no taste (unknown brand, probably the cheapest). Overall, the kitchen is a disaster. The ladies at the reception were unfortunately not from Poland, many things were not understandable to them… Moreover, the swimming pool and jacuzzi are closed, which we only find out after paying for the reservation, which is very poor.

title: Passable
neg_text: One key for the entire wardrobe. No glasses in the room. At the request of the receptionist I heard" they will be when I find them." Great that it worked out. I don't think anyone has ever cleaned under the bed. The day before we spent at Qubus Kraków was much better. Apart from the location and delicious breakfast, I do not recommend it. Drama room. No one informed us about the renovation on the first floor and the work that had been going on since the morning. The gym is located at the swimming pool. Literally.

title: Due to its central location, it is suitable for a short stay.
pos_text:

The head chef was very nice and creative, and the evening menu tasted very good.
neg_text:

The menu selection in the evening was very limited, especially since we had expected a buffet.
The drink prices were clearly too high (beer 0.5 l for €6.70)

title: This hotel could be much better, with very little effort. Tea bags, coffee and milk in the bedrooms (and a spoon). Get a
pos_text: Excellent location, clean rooms, carpets looked very tired and worn.
neg_text:
Pillows on the bed were useless way too soft, had to use a folded up dressing gown for support. There’s a kettle in the room, but no tea, coffee, milk, sugar or even a spoon.
Breakfast can be chaotic, not enough capacity for the number of guests. Plenty of selection of pastries, cold meats, cheeses, juices, etc. Toaster like most hotels, completely useless , and only four slots when there are about 50+ guests. Most just gave up.
Bacon usually cold unless you got a fresh batch, same with scrambled eggs. They cooked me two fresh fried eggs which was a massive improvement.

title: If you want to get any sleep at all DO NOT STAY at this hotel!
pos_text: Pleasant staff, great location, good breakfast
neg_text:
Extremely loud outside! Did not get any sleep the 2 nights we stayed there. Extremely loud cars and motorcycles racing all night long starting at 7 pm (1900) I swear they were going back and forth on the street in front of us all night and along with that the people in the bar near us were extremely loud all night long. There is no air in the room, we had one window open to get air, we closed it because of all this of the noise, it didnt quiet the noise and our room got extremely hot and stuffy. The noise did not stop until 7 am. The pillows were ridiculous, one pillow per person and they were as flat as a pancake when you laid your head on them.
The elevator is old, there’s a big dent in the doors, sometimes it would not open or close completely., we had to help it!

title: Wrocław is a beautiful city, but staying in this hotel spoiled the good impression.
pos_text: Breakfast is one big disaster! We ate three breakfasts, so we have a comparison. Every time it was the same and what was supposed to be warm - scrambled eggs and sausages - was cold. I don't even want to mention it.
neg_text: I didn't like practically everything. From the deception that the hotel has parking, when in fact it does not have any parking, through a room with damaged windows, to a hopeless breakfast and unpleasant service.

title: Very poor

neg_text: The room is dark, the windows are from another era, the furniture is painted, the paint has worn off after washing, in some places it's ugly and gloomy, like in a scary mansion, the windows can't be opened because downstairs there are guests sitting and talking until 2 a.m. and the echo carries, tuned vehicles drive down the street, you can't go to sleep, it's just a scary mansion, only there are no ghosts.

There is no restaurant in the hotel, you have to go outside. Leaky windows overlooking an alley with restaurants. Even though it's a bit noisy during the season, I can't imagine sleeping there in the summer. The hotel is 4*, but in my opinion it deserves 2. The price is excessive.. The owner sits in the hall and observes the guests.

neg_text: Our room was very, very small. The open suitcase had to be laid out on the floor due to lack of space. The room was not cleaned or the beds made. When we inquired, we were told that we should have mentioned this at check-in. However, we were not informed of that. Given the high price paid, an uncleaned room is unacceptable!

title: Worst hotel in a long time
pos_text:
Breakfast was very tasty with freshly prepared scrambled eggs. The staff here were also very courteous.
The proximity to the city center is great.
neg_text:
The journey was difficult because the alleged parking spaces do not exist. Eight pieces for the entire hotel means we got nothing. Unfortunately, I only found out after booking.
The lady at the reception was unfriendly and spoke poor English and no German at all, which made communication difficult.
It's best to use the elevator only in an emergency, but it's still better than your room.
We were accommodated under the roof. It was uncomfortably warm, but the noise outside made it impossible to open a window. Every door squeaked, the beds creaked. We both had sore backs the next day; it was the most restless night of our entire vacation.
The bathroom is in keeping with the hotel, but the shower is too small for an adult and the water pressure is terrible.
There was also a useless television.

title: Compared to other 4-star hotels, this one performed very poorly.

Clearly overpriced accommodation. Don't stay here.

title: Poor

title: If someone likes sleeping in an ice hotel, this will be a good choice.
pos_text: Location. Breakfast ok.
neg_text: Well, yes, I chose the hotel based on repeated good experiences at the Qubus hotel in Krakow, which meets all 4* standards and is simply much better in every respect than the facility in Wrocław, in my opinion. The facility in Wrocław offers check-in from 3 p.m. What a surprise we had when we arrived at the hotel at 3:20 p.m. We saw the reception desk, hmm, honestly, in motels it is more representative than in the Qubus Hotel Wrocław, we proceed to check-in, unfortunately the room it turns out that the reception was not ready to offer us any coffee or anything while we were waiting for the room that was supposed to be waiting for us... okay, after a few minutes the room is ready... We dropped our luggage in the room and went into town to take care of things. After returning home, the room was cold, 18 degrees Celsius, no, it wasn't because of the open windows or something, it was just 18 degrees Celsius in the room... We set the airflow to 23 degrees and unfortunately, under the duvet, the airflow was terribly cold... so I had to turn it off, hide under the covers, put on a hat and try to sleep. In the morning, the temperature in the room was around 16 degrees. I didn't test the pool, even though this was one of the factors that determined my choice of hotel, but if it was also 16 degrees there, I preferred to skip it... Breakfast was okay, although compared to Qubus Kraków, it was poor... When we told the reception that the room temperature was 16 degrees, we were told that the heating season had not started yet (the temperature was 2 degrees at night). Well, generally speaking, just as I have always recommended the facility in Krakow, I will definitely advise against the facility in Wrocław because the location does not compensate for the rest, especially considering the price of less than PLN 700 per night.

title: NOT MY FAVORITE
pos_text: Delicious and rich breakfast. During my 3 nights I wasn't able to try everything. Very good coffee. Tasty food in the restaurant. Friendly reception staff and very professional in the restaurant. Hotel in the center of the Old Town - close to everything. Swimming pool and jacuzzi on site and possibility of using spa treatments. Night security guard (older man) very empathetic, polite and helpful. Water in dispensers on each floor instead of bottles - a very nice and eco-friendly idea. Comfortable beds. The room has a fridge and a balcony. Low price for the standard and location.
neg_text: A 100 PLN voucher to be used in the hotel restaurant does not compensate for the mess and dirt that was in the room. Night lamps - layer of dust. Contacts - last washed half a year ago. The bathroom is full of hair from the previous tenants. Pubic hair in the bidet. YUCK!!! Pieces of glass in the bedsheets. After reporting the problem - it was not fixed. The bed linen was not changed. The hair remained. After another intervention - the bathroom is so-so. The bedding is still the same and it wasn't even laid out as part of the service. These are not 4-star hotel standards. The water in the pool, which is supposed to be recreational, is freezing cold. It's definitely not 27 degrees as it says on the board. The jacuzzi is dirty - you can see it by the color of the foam. As part of the training, I suggest sending employees to Qubus in Krakow or Gdansk. There, 4 stars mean a 4-star standard, not a low-quality motel like in Wrocław. Never again. I do not recommend it.

title: Very good
pos_text:
The hotel is a short walk from the main square, which is a big advantage. They were very pleasant at the reception. Our room was modern, medium sized. You can refill your water for free in the hallway at any time (incl. soda) from the vending machine.
We visited the wellness center in the evening. We ended up using everything the hotel had to offer, but perfection was lacking in fine-tuning the details.
You can also swim in the pool, but the water was very cold.
The hot tub is in the corner of the pool, ideally for 4 people, so you have to be lucky to get in. It was pleasant and the water was warm.
The sauna is right next to the pool, it is very small, max. for 4 people. Because of its location, people go there in swimsuits.
It's a shame that there are almost no storage spaces or hangers in this one large room. There are only 3 chairs to relax, that's really not enough (but I understand - there's no room there).
The advantage is that there is a water dispenser, towels and possibly spare slippers.
The overall impression of the wellness center depends a lot on how many other people you meet there.
I have to praise the breakfast a lot - a large selection, you can also make fresh juice, good coffee, there is a waffle machine and I recommend trying the cheeses, they are very good.
neg_text:
I would have reservations about the non-functioning safe (but we didn't address it), the bed mattress is very soft and we expected that there would be cream/body lotion available in the bathroom (there was only liquid soap/shower gel). Otherwise, everything you need is here.
The wellness area is smaller and everything is in one place - don't expect privacy or comfort here unless you're very lucky.

At Scandic, a certain level is generally guaranteed: the good and varied breakfast was not much worse than what I know from the Scandinavian hotels of this chain, and in terms of comfort, finishing and bathroom equipment it was perhaps even better. The clever layout of the room deserves praise. An additional plus for the helpfulness of the staff - we checked in without any problems, although it was technically over an hour before the scheduled check-in time.
neg_text: The main problem: the promised soundproofing is unfortunately a lie. You can hear quite a lot from the busy Piłsudskiego Street: from accelerating cars to all possible sounds of trams (which have a stop right in front of the window) there were loud conversations and laughter of passers-by. Room decor is a more subjective thing, but I admit that the yellowish wallpaper, which looked like it hadn't been renovated in decades, put me in a rather depressing mood. Sleeping was not made easier by the pillows being too soft: to quote his wife, "to get a good night's sleep, you need pillows that you can rest your head on, not ones you sink into."

Silence and peace to rest at night.
neg_text: The bed mattress is very soft, you can just sink into it and not roll over. A separate discussion about breakfast - it's not bad, serving the standard set of scrambled eggs, sausages, English breakfast accessories, as well as pancakes, several types of snacks and dessert. The bad thing is that we went for breakfast twice around 8:30, and both times the hot food Marmites were empty! After checking in, we were given a key to the room where a STRANGE MAN was staying. The lights were off and the whole situation was very stressful and, above all, dangerous for us, especially since we were two women.
The staff only told us that they had no idea who this man was and that they had assigned us a different room which was not in accordance with the reservation (instead of a double bed there were two single beds). and we were told that nothing could be done and we had to push the beds together. When I asked for any compensation or attempt to solve the problem, I was informed that I had to submit a handwritten complaint, which I did and I was given a ridiculous 10% discount on my next stay because ACCORDING TO THEM, NOTHING LIKE THAT HAPPENED. Additionally, the breakfasts differed from the offer, and when we tried to cancel, we were also met with a complete lack of assistance. I am attaching their pathetic response and my complaint. At breakfast, the staff took the plate away from under my nose without asking if it was okay. Speaking of breakfast - I have never seen a worse hotel breakfast in my life. The scrambled eggs looked as if they had been vomited, the sausages and hot dogs were dripping with grease, and the pastry looked old. When I tried to pour juice, colored water came out. Only the bread and fruit were quite okay. Additionally, there are free elevators, for which the wait can be up to 5 minutes during busy hours. My wife was also hit by the elevator door, which apparently did not have a working sensor - she suffered a bruise on her hand and the phone she was holding flew in an arc to the floor. The entrance to the hotel is terrible, and the location is such that you have to drive everywhere anyway. I do not recommend this hotel unless you are only interested in a comfortable bed.

Breakfast takes place in a very small room and the dishes are packed on the bar. The larger room had some closed event, so the ladies were not allowed in from 9 a.m. as a precaution, the entire hotel was packed into this one room. The selection and standard of dishes were just as good as the rest :(. I am a big fan of this chain but the price is like in any other hotel and unfortunately the standard is not. I think that in this budget you can find something more interesting in Wrocław.

title: Perfect for a weekend stay in Wrocław
pos_text: Very clean rooms. An incredibly comfortable mattress to sleep on. Blackout curtains - they do not let any light through, so if you are a light sleeper, you don't have to worry. Fresh, fragrant bedding. Clean and spacious (although not large) bathroom, fully equipped - towels, cosmetics, washing gel, shampoo and a set for women (this is a huge plus!). Coffee, tea and chocolate available 24/7 downstairs in the lobby. The decor of the entire hotel is well thought out, very aesthetic, and pleasing to the eye. The location is a huge plus, right in the center of the city.
neg_text: If someone is a sensitive sleeper, it is worth asking for a room at the end of the corridor, with a window overlooking the courtyard. It was our first time at Puro and we got a room with a window facing the street, and since our stay was on a weekend, we could hear all the parties, garbage trucks, people returning from parties in the morning, and people talking in the hotel corridor at night. The price of the hotel parking lot does not encourage its use. A few steps from the hotel there is the NFM parking lot, underground, but cheaper. The prices at the bar downstairs by the hotel reception are high, not matching the room prices, and they also add a commission for up to two drinks. There was no information about this on the menu or before entering the card into the terminal. I realized it after the load. There is no fridge in the lowest standard rooms. For a short, weekend stay this place is absolutely fine.

When choosing this hotel, I was guided by the option of a swimming pool and jacuzzi. Unfortunately, if someone wants to warm up in the jacuzzi after a long day, there is no such option because the water in the jacuzzi is the temperature of a swimming pool. It is constructed in such a way that water from the pool overflows into the jacuzzi. If I had known this, I don't know if I would have chosen this hotel because it was crucial for me as I am not a fan of saunas.
Another issue is the lighting in the rooms... Unfortunately, if someone wants to get ready to go out after sunset, it is very troublesome because despite turning on all possible lights, the room is still dark... And there are a lot of shadows.
As a mood option it's very nice because this creates a cozy evening mood. However, for me it was definitely too dark... when I wanted to reach for clothes from the closet, I had to use my phone's flashlight to see what I was reaching for, and when it comes to makeup, unfortunately, in the semi-darkness and full of shadows, I couldn't see anything. This is a curiosity for women.

2. Unfortunately, there was a party above my room until 1 a.m. The staff did not react, although the party often moved to the corridor and there was shouting and slamming of doors 😥 3. Minor shortcomings include a broken shower cabin door and the TV not being turned on via the remote control (tolerable). 😁 4. The worst part of my stay was the morning surprise during settlement. The lady at the reception asked if I had used the bar in the fridge! Since there was something there, I assumed that the hotel staff knew how to take care of the customer and it was a treat. Nothing could be further from the truth. The receptionist added up my bill and charged me over 100 PLN (she added it up on a piece of paper, without a receipt)! I guess you should also charge for toilet paper, tea, soap, etc. If you are going to engage in such "fraud", it would be worth informing the customer that what is in the fridge is paid for! 😥😥😥 Overall impression. Nice hotel, although too focused on profit! I understand that times are hard, but to this extent?

title: I recommend it, but there are some drawbacks
pos_text:
The best advantage of the hotel is breakfast. The price for breakfast is quite high, but worth it all. The breakfasts were very good, fresh and there was everything you could want: granola, various types of yogurt, muesli, milk, fresh fruit, juicer, fruit juices, scrambled eggs, eggs, bacon, baked vegetables, omelets, cheese, blue and blue cheeses, olives, various salads, cold cuts, butter, coffee, tea. Vegetarian zone: vegan cheese, cold cuts, cheese, plant-based sausages, plant-based milk. Chia, tapioca. For coffees, teas, ginger, lemon, white sugar, brown sugar, sweeteners. Chocolate cream, maple syrup. Still, sparkling, cold water. Really… there is everything. Additionally, there is the option of breakfast boxes, which are really very filling. There are also highchairs for children in the breakfast restaurant, so that's very nice. Reception. This is also a huge plus. Nice people and in fact, they solve every problem and whim. We wanted a non-slip mat, we got it. We wanted a candle, we got it. And other things too, which brings us to another advantage… Housekeeping. The maids are wonderful and do a good job. We asked that all glass items and decorations be removed from the room before arrival, as we have small children and we did not want them damaged. And everything was hidden and secured. When cleaning was ordered, it was obvious that it was clean and quick. Women really do make it. Eco-friendly hotel. There are waste sorting bins in the rooms. They do not clean rooms every day, only upon request. At breakfast they make smoothies from the remaining fruit. All products are eco and healthy. Additionally, you can take coffee to go with breakfast. They offer paper cups, which is a huge plus, because you usually can't take anything out of the kitchen. There is a small “shop” in the lobby where you can buy everything you need. Tampons, pads, razor, shampoo, body lotion, and snacks + coffee machine. Prices are high, but things are needed.
The Stockholm restaurant, although open at low hours, has nice service. Delicious drinks. Soundproof rooms.
neg_text:
-There was a huge luggage trolley at the entrance to the hotel, but no one offered to take our luggage, even though we had a lot of luggage.
-At the very bottom there is the Stockholm restaurant, which is unfortunately open from Tuesday to Saturday and from 5 p.m. This is very problematic because breakfast is until 10:00, so I have to wait until 17:00 for dinner.
-Fitness room (gym and sauna) is located on the first floor, next to the conference rooms. To get there you have to go through the corridor through all the conference rooms. Which may be uncomfortable when you want to go, for example, to the gym already dressed.
Hotel Room (standard family room)
-The bathroom is missing a single light. When you want to use the toilet. e.g. at night, all the big lights come on at once.
-The bathroom lacks a towel hook, e.g. to your hands so that it is at hand.
-The bathroom has a bathtub with the option of a shower. There is a glass pane that is placed in an unfortunate place because you cannot turn on the water through the glass.
-The basket in the bathroom is very small.
-There is a bin in the room for sorting, but it is very small. With 4 people in the room, we had to ask for extra garbage disposal every day because we didn't fit.
-We had one large bed and one sofa bed. This sofa was a disaster. It's scary for an adult to sit there, let alone sleep, because every time they turn around, the whole thing moves and squeaks. It's impossible to sleep.
-A 4* hotel should have room service as standard, but unfortunately it doesn't. What a pity.
-The fridge is very small and not normal, it only keeps the temperature a little.
-There is no WI-FI on the TV, you can't start e.g. Netflix. Just regular television.
-The carpet is so colored that it appears very dirty.
-The air conditioning worked as it should and was very loud. Additionally, it was written that the air conditioning would turn off automatically when the window was opened - this did not happen.

Breakfast is disorganized and chaotic, with empty food platters that are not constantly replenished by the staff.
During breakfast, it's hard to find something to eat because there's often no bread, cheese, or cold cuts... the waiters also don't care about cleanliness in the restaurant, and the tables are littered with dirty plates and cups from previous guests.

The rooms have seen their best years, the furniture is a bit worn out and the cleanliness wasn't perfect either. However, for such a short stay the impressions were ok.

title: Pleasant

neg_text:
Late check-in from 15.00, you cannot pay in advance for your stay if you arrive a little earlier than 15, it is a bit inconvenient if someone comes from far away and wants to explore the area. 20 minutes walk to the center is a plus.
Unfortunately, our expectations were much higher for a 5* hotel, I admit that I have personally been to better 5* hotels. First of all, on the first day there was no shower gel because whoever gave us the room did not check it. And I admit that my husband was very surprised when he went to take a shower, and here was such a surprise. Breakfast was poor for a 5* hotel, and many of the items looked stale: from the previous day, the fruit was overripe, and the vegetables were wilted. Please forgive me, but such situations should not occur in a 5* hotel. The check-in staff was inexperienced and did not provide specific answers. The staff in the restaurant were very helpful. For 1 night this hotel is not worth the price, there are several other hotels closer to the center with a better standard.

You can sleep well on the beds. But they can certainly be more comfortable. The rooms are no different in quality and size from similar hotel chains.

The hotel itself is not bad. However, I have to compare it to others I have visited recently.
In this regard, breakfasts lag behind in both selection and quality.
The food at the restaurant is delicious, but it's missing something that would make me leave feeling like it was a culinary experience.
I was a little bothered by the shower. It is too economical, so you don't get a satisfactory shower.
Otherwise, just an average hotel that won't impress, but thanks to the brand you can be sure that you won't be disappointed.

When the deposit was finally returned, it seems to me that the room where my personal belongings were left, including my work phone, should have been checked first.
Shameful behavior for a 5-star hotel that no one informed me about finding it, as it turned out 10 minutes after my check-out that the phone had been left at the hotel so I could come back for it. Only late in the evening, when I was home, did I notice that I did not have my phone. I called the staff and asked if it had been found and that due to the incident with another guest, they simply "forgot to inform me". How can a hotel of this caliber and with accolades everywhere allow such situations to occur in 2026? Every visit there is the same problem with the young people at the reception. Does anyone managing the facility know about this?

title: Beautiful hotel and facilities, but unfortunately the service and staff attitude were far below the standards expected f
pos_text: Platinum Palace Boutique Hotel & SPA is a very elegant and well-maintained hotel, with spacious rooms and a pleasant atmosphere. neg_text:
Unfortunately, the experience was affected by several issues that I did not expect from a hotel of this standard. The staff did not seem adequately trained for a 5-star hotel and, at times, appeared not prepared to manage a luxury property. My room was not cleaned properly during the stay, it was left untidy, and I felt poorly treated when I reported the issue.

I appreciated that the management eventually agreed to a partial refund through Booking.com, but I still believe the compensation was not fully proportionate to the inconvenience experienced. Overall, the hotel has excellent potential thanks to the structure and facilities, but the level of service and guest care needs significant improvement to truly meet 5-star expectations.

- leaking shower with missing door seal. Additionally, the door is either damaged or has dropped, which is why the other seals are not closing properly. After every bath, there is just a puddle in front of the shower

- bathroom tiles moving on the floor when you step on them, or a damaged toilet seat, or rather its mounting.

- traces of mold in the shower cabin, the shower faucet is so calcified that it is difficult to set the water temperature

- bathroom fan not working, bathroom door handle loose,

- leaky window, although there are external blinds and it can be somewhat prevented from getting cold air in, but it's weak.

- a worn out bed mattress to the point that when you sleep you just sink in the middle

- no garbage can, except for a mini garbage can in the bathroom. There are tea cups and a kettle - but no spoons or anything to stir the tea with :)

The room is tiny and has "tenants" in the form of ants crawling around the bathroom and the room. After reporting the issue, the room was not changed, but "cleaned". On the same day in the evening, a similar situation occurred, i.e. the "tenants" returned. We were promised a room change the next day when another room became available. The next day in the afternoon we were given a room change but this time on the noisy side facing the main street towards the center, the windows seemed to be missing, the noise and traffic seemed to be constantly open. Sleeping pillows are like flat "pancakes", so sleeping problems and neck pain are guaranteed. The breakfast was very poor and meager, and the cleanliness was questionable. The bathroom had a bathtub the size of a baby's bathtub, and there was no socket in the bathroom, so you couldn't plug in your curling iron or hairdryer. The TV cannot connect to the internet, so if you have streaming services, forget about using them.
The hotel parking lot is a joke, tight, small, and the worst part is that if you buy a space and go out into the city, you can't be sure there will be a space for you when you return. Parking is prohibited on the streets around and the city guard is often present. THE HOTEL IS A FAILURE, WE WILL DEFINITELY NEVER GO BACK THERE.

pos_text:
One of the best hotels I've ever stayed in.
Amazing location, in the city center, next to the river, yet a very quiet street, not noisy whatsoever! Next to lots of public transport stops and the bus from the airport.

Very clean, very modern, comfortable rooms with everything you might need. The beds are super comfortable.

The room is "smart", you can control the TV, lights, temple, set an alarm, contact reception and many more things via it. You can even screencast your phone to the TV.

The breafast is great, doesn't vary day to day but there is enough variety to not get bored and everything was delicious.

Free coffee, tee, hot chocolate and apples, all dag and night, which is a great bonus.

Lots of useful services at the hotel, they can even get you a PlayStation if you request it (didn't try that but it was in the hotel info).

Didn't try the restaurant but it was priced very decently.

Very kind and helpful staff.

Overall, just an amazing experience and I'd love to come back!

The only thing is that I am not a fan of the "open" bathrooms with glass but they offer a curtain to cover this part of the room so you can have some privacy.

Also a second mirror besides the one in the bathroom would be great.

title: Customer deception - Only 1 night - then moved to another hotel

neg_text:
The images displayed for the bookable rooms on booking.de are incorrect. All room categories are displayed with the same photos. However, we only noticed this after check-in, after we were disappointed with the room. We had a room with a balcony/I booked a terrace room and discovered that there are no rooms of that type. However, if the room description mentions "terrace", we expect one to be present. We complained, but unfortunately the hotel staff showed no understanding. The only thing they say is "we have no rooms with balconies" - so why is it being advertised? We were offered the paved courtyard (with unclean seating) as a terrace.
The fact is: The photos from booking.de are not actual room photos (the photos we have included are supposed to show a "room with a king-size bed"). A complete deception!!!!!! The bed was 1.40 m wide!!
The next morning (breakfast was rather poor), we booked a different hotel in Wrocław and moved. Never again Dikul Market

title: An unpleasant experience
pos_text: Location. The location is absolutely ok.
neg_text: In the room we were given, the bed had broken boards. It's a shame that no one did anything about it when the room was being prepared before my arrival, and it was certainly visible after removing the bedding. We only noticed it when we went to bed, but since it was late we decided we would somehow get through the night on that bed. The next morning I reported the bed to the reception. I asked for a room change. Unfortunately, the lady at the reception said she couldn't change the room because the hotel was 100% booked, so I suggested changing the hotel. The lady said she would contact the hotel manager and call him, and she noted the bed failure. After reporting, we left the hotel. No one called during the day, so we assumed that perhaps the bed had been repaired or replaced, or that a place in another room had become available. Imagine our surprise when we returned to the hotel in the evening and found that nothing had been done about our case. The bed was just as broken before we left as it was when we returned. My second intervention at the reception desk and a conversation with the manager resulted in the bed being replaced. Of course, when I first reported it at the reception desk, the lady didn't inform anyone about it, let alone the manager. However, the hotel showed no sense of guilt, no compensation - not even in the form of a free breakfast, not even a simple apology from the receptionist who ignored the matter. I know that 3 stars is not 5, but still, some standard is expected from a 3-star hotel, especially in such an important matter. On my part, there is still a bad taste in my mouth, I will definitely avoid this hotel on future trips.
