title: A stay that somehow managed to be smooth, comfortable, and unexpectedly entertaining.
pos_text:
Every hotel stay blends into the next after a while. Same rooms, same breakfasts, same “it was lovely” reviews that mean absolutely nothing.

This one didn’t.

Not because of anything dramatic. Quite the opposite. It just quietly got everything right. Check-in was smooth, the room was exactly what you want after travelling, and nothing felt like a compromise. No noise, no faff, no “why is this like this?” moments.

But the highlight? Breakfast theatre.

On our second morning, a man arrived in full hotel robe, like he’d taken a wrong turn out of a spa and decided to commit to the look. Fair enough. Except he then proceeded to pace the lobby like a minor celebrity avoiding paparazzi, surveying the room with the kind of calm authority usually reserved for people who own islands.

Nobody stopped him. Nobody questioned it. And somehow, it worked. The whole place carried on as if this was completely normal behaviour.

That moment summed the hotel up better than anything else. Comfortable enough that people relax into it, but polished enough that even a bathrobe power-walk through reception doesn’t break the atmosphere.

Beyond the unexpected entertainment, everything else delivered exactly as it should. Great location for exploring Wrocław, genuinely good breakfast, and staff who manage to be helpful without feeling scripted.

It’s rare to find somewhere that just works without trying too hard.

We’ll be back. And next time, I might bring a robe just to see how far I can push it.
neg_text: The only minor issue was the free candy at reception. A great idea, unless you arrive with children who immediately identify it as a core feature of the hotel. From that point on, leaving or entering the building required a negotiation I was rarely winning.

title: I DO NOT RECOMMEND!!!! PLEASE READ THE DESCRIPTION!!!!!
pos_text: During a two-day stay at this hotel, the first day was ok, the service was very nice. Towels are changed regularly at the customer's request, cleaning products, coffee, tea and toilet paper are provided.
neg_text: During my second night at the hotel, in a rented apartment, I experienced an unpleasant adventure. Namely, in the morning while sleeping my bed collapsed (cornea) on which I slept when the children slept on the double bed. I immediately informed the hotel staff about this, expressed my dissatisfaction with this situation, asking for an appropriate approach to the customer in such a situation, taking into account the quite high cost of renting the apartment. After informing the hotel staff, I was informed that the owner of the facility would be informed about this situation and that the facility's cleaner would inspect the bed. Unfortunately, after 6 a.m., I was forced to return to the apartment without the possibility of further rest. On the floor where my apartment was located, a cleaning lady was working around 9 o'clock and I asked her to inspect the bed, which had collapsed. After a long time, after the cleaning lady had inspected it, I managed to put the damaged hinge from the extendable part of the cornea back in place and it was possible to fold it or unfold it again. At approximately 9:15 a.m., I was informed by the hotel staff at the reception that, after speaking with the owner, I would be charged PLN 400 for damage to the bed. During this conversation I also received information that the cornea is not intended for sleeping for adults. After informing the hotel staff that they would refer the case to a lawyer for treating me and my family in such a way, that a room with a damaged bed was rented for me and my family, endangering my health which I still feel in my lower back and that I had to pay for the repair of the already damaged bed. After this conversation, after a dozen or so minutes, I was informed by the hotel staff that the hotel owner would not want to charge me for the cost of repairing the damaged cornea.

pos_text: Location - nothing more
neg_text:
In the photos the hotel looks stylish and charming, but in reality it looks like a workers' hostel or an old hospital. The owner defended himself by claiming that these were “illustrative photos” and there may be a difference, but for me it is a scam and misleading guests. title: Windows that don't open, noisy airflow and stuffy air at night
pos_text: If you have a car, there is good transport connection to the Old Town (10-15 minutes via the Warsaw Bridge). Nearby there is a KFC restaurant and a BP gas station. Hotel guests can use the small swimming pool free of charge, although only during specific hours (during our stay it was from 4:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m.).
neg_text:
- The biggest disadvantage is the non-opening windows (probably to save on fire insurance) and an unreliable ventilation system that malfunctions if other guests on the same floor have their ventilation options set incorrectly. This caused quite a stuffy atmosphere on the first night, with hot air pouring in from the unopened window and the room temperature ranging between 25 and 32 degrees Celsius. C. The loud airflow turned off by itself after 2-3 minutes. It was already quiet at night and the facility staff were unable to check the settings in other guests' rooms. As a result of the intervention, we received an office fan and a promise that it would be repaired the next morning. To survive until morning, we had to sleep with the door ajar, risking having our luggage stolen.

- Extra paid parking, parking at the BP gas station is risky (only allowed up to max. 30 minutes under threat of towing).
- Equipment such as a kettle, glasses, cutlery or garbage bags are not standard, but you can easily ask for them at the reception.
- The pool is a bit small, designed for a maximum of 6 people. Then it gets too crowded and competitive swimming, especially crawl, becomes impossible for fear of hitting other guests.

- Breakfast additionally payable PLN 45 - standard offer without fireworks, fine for max. 3 mornings. Then, due to the repetition of the offer, monotony creeps in. There are no large plates, so you have to go to the buffet several times with small plates.

He informed us that it started working properly now, but that we shouldn't have it on full speed because it gives cold air (????) and he set it on low speed. Half an hour later the air coming from the a/c was cold. I called again the reception to let them know, and the girl of the reception told me that her colleague informed her that it will take some time and we should wait 15-30 minutes. Half an hour later I went down to the reception, asked the girl to go and check it on her self that the air coming it's cold. She insisted that she just spoke again with her colleague which informed her that the room's temperature just started increasing and it will be warmer and warmer.
After we spent a couple of hours in the room and waited the temperature to get warmer, we asked them to change us the room. They said that they don't have another room and they brought us a small fan heater. The bathroom was cold throughout the whole night, the fan heater was very very noise and we had to close it too at night, leaving the room very cold.
The worst part is that I informed them that this is going to be a bad review for their hotel and they couldn't care less. They lied from the beginning as they knew that their heating is having issues in all the building (I have read some reviews afterwards from other customers), they made us spend 2 hours inside the room to wait for the heating to start, even though we only had 1 day to see the city.
At the end we preferred to sleep in a cold room without the noise of the fan heater.
I do not recommend this hotel to anyone. Totally unprofessional people. The worst 150€ we spent on a single night.

The pool is really great, large and empty, the water was very warm. The kids were delighted 🙂 It's clean. Towels and bathrobes white and fluffy.
The rooms are spacious.

Only capsule coffee for breakfast, but also very good teas.

title: Would not stay again.. many other accomodations in better locations/value for money
pos_text:
The old building that has been renovated to create this hotel.
The staff were friendly, one lady was particularly helpful (the lady on front desk the next morning after our arrival)
neg_text:
Many things.
* BIG CONCERN.. we had ordered an Uber one morning for 6:30am pick up. When we arrived down stairs and waited inside for the Uber to arrive, we realised the front door was locked (large wooden doors). There was no button to open them or any information how to open them. I become quite panicked and was trying everything to get outside. It was a terrible feeling to feel trapped with no idea of how to get out.
It was then a young lady came and unlocked the door with a large key.
(Big concern as there are no fire evacuation instructions or any emergency instructions (not in polish or English to be seen anywhere).

* Definitely not catered for the English speaking traveler (not one tv channel catered for in English speaking, not even the ability to use subtitles)
* Location: while in an okay location it is NOT on the old town market square. It is on the very outer of the old town and about 5 large blocks (walking) to the actual market square.
* Two sachets of tea, two sachets of coffee left for one week staying at this property. NO milk. When asked for Milk they said they did not have any. We had to buy our own.
* House cleaning is at an extra charge (we were fortunate to get 1 free clean due to the length of our stay. This should be mentioned in their profile.
* As the room was not serviced daily the dirt and dust and hair built up quickly on the floorboards. It become quite dirty to walk around in. A broom and dustpan would have been useful. The rooms are large and a lot of floor space.
*No hand towels left and had to use the bath mat to wipe hands on. Towels were old and ratty on the ends.
* When asking for One extra pillow, they said they didn’t know if they had any??? 24 hours later they delivered one.

We booked 7nights stay and left on day 5. We realised there were far better value for money stays, closer to the market square and with better facilities/amenities.

title: Adequate. I would return.
pos_text: The staff was very friendly. The cleaning lady did not ignore our request not to enter the room. There was a safe in the room. The hotel was very quiet and the bed was very comfortable and clean.
neg_text:
I didnt like that i had to pay extra for parking my car.

I also didnt like that some days there was breakfast, and some days there wasnt and you had to 'order' your breakfast. It might not matter to most people, but when youre travelling with children it is the high point of the kids day, to see the buffet and get to choose what they want. HOWEVER, on days that there was breakfast, it was very good and tasty.

I also didnt like the extra stuffed animals in the room. My children travel with their own stuffed animals. And stuffed animals are notorious for being bug infested. I dont want my kids cuddling up to some stuffed animal whose history I dont know. Its kind of disgusting to be honest. BUT: we really loved the little princess castle in the room. The girls LOVED that. So keep the princess castle but get rid of the mangy old stuffed animals :)

Very loud music and noise from bar in courtyard opposite my room. No sleep for 2 nights until another room was provided on other side of hotel which was far quieter.
Very unfriendly breakfast waiter. A smile costs nothing!

title: A great introduction for us to Poland. Recommend it!
pos_text: All aspects. Traditional, well maintained, very comfortable and well situated accommodation. Staff all very friendly. Breakfast very good.
neg_text: Nothing of any materiality to share.

title: Superb
pos_text: There was a large selection for breakfast, both savory and sweet. There was water, juices, coffee - black and cappuccino, various teas available. The reception is available 24/7, a big plus for me - there is a filter dispenser for water in the hallway - both sparkling and still. If someone doesn't like tap water, this is a great solution. Additionally, there were jugs and glasses in the rooms, so there was no risk of creating waste and buying bottled water. We had 3 rooms together as a group, one of which didn't have brand new mattresses and beds, they were a little uncomfortable, but nothing terrible. The rooms were generally quite spacious and nicely furnished.
neg_text: My friends and I had 3 rooms together and in one the beds were not very comfortable, one creaked. the second room had a better (newer) bed and overall the room was much larger (for the same price as ours)

title: Simple hotel in a prime location

neg_text: The beds and the cleanliness

title: 10 with honors
pos_text: Fantastic hotel, beautiful lobby, very large room, spotlessly clean, super comfortable, comfy beds, TV with Italian channels, free water dispensers on every floor. Bus stop 1 minute away, 10 minutes' walk from the station and about 10-15 minutes' walk from the center. We didn't have breakfast but we had dinner in the restaurant which satisfied us. Excellent value for money.
neg_text: In our room we couldn't open the windows but it wasn't a problem because the ventilation system was excellent.

title: Good
pos_text: Cleanliness, water from the dispenser in the corridor
neg_text: No ventilation in the room, having to sleep with the window open. Shower door not closing properly

title: Very good
pos_text: delicious breakfasts, close by car to the center, comfortable beds.
neg_text: It would be good to get a detailed route in advance on how to get to the hotel. In bad weather, especially at night, arriving at the main entrance and hearing that you have to turn around and go around the building is not convenient.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Location
neg_text: Not clean and expensive for one night only

title: EXCELLENT, I highly RECOMMEND it.
pos_text:
The hotel is very well located, in the center of Wrocław, near the Market Square and very well connected to the city's transport system. The hotel facilities, common areas and rooms are in excellent condition. The truth is, the photos don't lie. The beds are comfortable and the shower is spacious, as is the bathroom. The location allows you to enjoy Wroclaw's nightlife without any problems, and during the day you can easily get away to other points of interest or simply explore its museums and historical sites.
The breakfast is EXCELLENT, it has VARIETY and it's FEELING. The food is excellent, and the service from the waiters, as well as the tableware used, is impeccable. The truth is, I was pleasantly surprised.
neg_text: Due to its excellent service, I could not stay longer at the hotel, as I could only stay for ONE (1) DAY, and it does not have its own parking. These are the only TWO (2) observations I can make.

title: I will NOT visit this hotel again
pos_text: Breakfast was OK
neg_text:
The water that comes out in the bathroom smells like sewage
The room key card didn't work half the time
And to top it all off, without any indication, it was suggested that I had smoked in the room, which I hadn't. The staff couldn't give me a reason why they came to that conclusion. That cost me a lot of unnecessary stress that I don't have on vacation!

title: Opening of the Christmas Market in Wrocław
pos_text: A pleasant hotel on the outskirts of Wrocław. About 5 km from the Old Town Square in the Krzyki district. You can get to the Center by tram line 7 and 17, stop on Przyjazni Street, 7 minutes from the hotel.Then it's about a 20 to 15 minute tram ride to the Opera stop and from there 7 minutes to the market square. It's hard to understand that everyone drives like that, even with small children. Option number two is Bolt, Uber, or Taxi. Comfortable rooms with a TV and a refrigerator. Hotel has a large parking lot for an additional fee. Drink bar in the lobby near the reception. Draught beer drinks coffee tea snacks. Nice people work there and it's clean.
neg_text: I liked everything, I'm generally satisfied. But I need to write something general. Prices for hotel stays increased dramatically from the end of November to the end of December due to the Christmas Markets. On weekends, of course. But this is in all hotels in Wrocław.

title: The hotel is very basic and uninviting, the breakfast is very unexciting, and the coffee is bad.
pos_text: The hotel is alright -- but completely lacking in charm, offering just the bare necessities. Not worth 3 stars.
neg_text: Afterwards, I stayed in a 3-star hotel in Opole, and it was like night and day in every respect, from the furnishings and comfort (of the bed) to the attentiveness of the hotel staff.

title: We would definitely stay here again.
pos_text: Superb location, right in the middle of the city. Easy walk to everything that you would want to see in this area.
neg_text: We had an oddball room that was located in what was likely the end of a hallway at one time in the past. It’s not that we didn’t like the room, it was just strange. There was a set of stairs in the den area leading to nowhere.

title: There are many things to improve.
pos_text: The location is really excellent, you can get around easily by tram and often on foot too.
neg_text:
Let me start by saying that there was a problem with the payment right from the start. While I was flying in the morning, the hotel had withdrawn the entire amount from my account when I had made the payment at the hotel. We later realized that this was a security measure, but this doesn't seem right to me, given that the agreements weren't like this, especially since no one ever told me that this was the procedure.
A few days earlier, they had already tried to withhold a similar, albeit lower, amount, and after requesting information through booking, the responses arrived, albeit rarely, but they weren't clear, and after a while, I didn't even receive a response.
In addition to this: toilet seat stained with urine NOT ours and even though I reported it as soon as I entered the room at reception they limited themselves to apologizing but no one cleaned it that same day or even the next day, I had to clean it myself.
The shower had no curtain or wall, directly on the floor, with the risk of a nearby hair dryer getting wet and causing serious injury. Towels were stained and few in number. We asked for face towels and were given three foot towels (I assume they were the bathmats with feet on them, as they're for the shower). Stained sheets and matching pillowcases were also included. The carpet on the floor was extremely dirty and inappropriate for such a space unless it was properly maintained. Drafts from the windows in the room, mold on adjacent walls, and splashes of who knows what on the brown ceiling. The bed and sheets were never changed or made during our stay.

title: Overall ok
pos_text: Clean rooms, daily cleaning, comfortable bed
neg_text: The hotel day starts at 2 p.m., at 2 p.m. we were told that our rooms were not cleaned and needed 15 minutes, after 15 minutes the receptionist had to actually ask the cleaning ladies to give us the room. If it weren't for her drive, we would probably have waited longer for the rooms. Plus, we were in a hurry, so we didn't have much time to wait. This is the first time we encounter something like this.

title: Overall a successful stay.
pos_text: The facility is located close to the old town. Good breakfast. Nice staff.
neg_text: We had a room on the ground floor adapted for a disabled person, even though none of us were disabled. It looked a bit better in the photos. The room faced the street, so you could hear the banging of suitcases dragged under the window all night. The bathroom stank of sewage.

title: Was very disappointed by selecting this hotel
neg_text:
3 bed room was way more spacious in photos than in reality. They have only one spacious 3-bed room that they use in photos, but other two rooms that had 3 beds were really small and beds were about half a meter apart.
Other rooms with 2 beds didn't have air conditioning. Was very hard to sleep because it was 32 degrees that day.
Hotel has huge parking lot, but you have to pay for parking.
Very bad coffee.

neg_text: Noise from the street - disturbing traffic of cars and trams. Cold in the room - very large windows and non-functioning heating/air conditioning controls. The exit from the underground car park is very steep and dangerous - you have to call the reception every time to make sure there is no car coming from the opposite direction.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Unfortunately the walls didn't insulate the room and we could hear the neighbor's television until late at night.

title: Passable

neg_text:
The wifi doesn't work and nothing has been done to fix it (we reported it to the reception three times).
At the reception they were unable to give directions to the city's attractions and there was not even a map available.
The structure is spartan, anonymous and does not stand out for the cleanliness of the rooms, nor, much less, is daily cleaning carried out.
At 5.30 in the morning it is light and there is no efficient darkening system in the room.
Very uncomfortable bed and pillow.
For breakfast I was offered desserts that were clearly not from the day (the same as the previous days).

The bidet was damaged and was flooding the bathroom. The staff solved it temporarily, but the bad taste remained.

However, we did get free breakfast as an apology and the lady at the reception was very nice.

Staff!

Yes, there was a problem with the air conditioning, but the staff did everything to make us comfortable.
The breakfast is also quite good. For three people, the race is very good.

The room is different from the photos

I hope that the comments will help raise the standard ;)

title: Perfect shot stay in perfect location for all I needed this tim in Wroclaw

title: It was a suitable accommodation for our purpose.
pos_text:
We went to a sports competition. The entrance to the Hala Orbita sports center is a half-minute walk from the hotel entrance. So in terms of location it was perfect for us /by the way, at least 15 minutes by taxi from the city center/.

The reception staff were very nice and helped with everything.

Free coffee and tea are available all day at the reception.
neg_text:
The room looked very clean, the smell was pleasant, but it was dirty under the bed and mattress.

The bed is not exactly the most comfortable, but acceptable.

The noise of cars can be heard strongly from the nearby overpass.

Breakfast is very expensive.

title: Holiday in a sanatorium
pos_text: Location partly
neg_text:
Unfortunately, the pictures, descriptions and reviews have nothing to do with what you actually find there.
The only great thing about the hotel is its location and the short walk to the city center and the market square. However, it's also noisy at night.

Otherwise, it resembles a sanatorium, or perhaps it was a retirement home.
The bed is so deep, it almost feels like sleeping on an air mattress. The room is large, so why are the beds so small? Only 1.40 meters wide!

Unfortunately, the bathroom is not a place where one feels comfortable at all. It's all age-appropriate, which is certainly helpful for some guests, but it has nothing to do with a nice bathroom in a hotel. The smell is unbearable and the towels are old, worn out and smell very unpleasant.

The elevator or the door is so extremely loud when opening that you can hear it all along the hallway.

The breakfast is good, but there is only one coffee machine, which also takes a very long time. Why not put up at least two? The breakfast room is small and too crowded; not everyone can find a seat. Unfortunately, it's also very uncomfortable.

All in all, the stay was unfortunately not relaxing. It was supposed to be a short break. The price is comparatively cheaper, but by no means justified. Half would have been more appropriate.

For us, this was our first and last stay there.

title: Disappointing
neg_text: broken lock, spent two nights with the door open

title: Passable

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The breakfast was plentiful and good, lots of different food options, lovely bread
neg_text: The room and bathroom smelled horrible.

title: Save your money. Stay elsewhere.
pos_text: The building is close to the old city.
neg_text:
This hotel was a disaster — they gave me a room, knowing that the AC doesn't work. I had to call the front desk after an hour of struggling to hear "oh, yes, your AC is broken, but you have ventilation". It took me a moment to realize that she meant the room had windows... and it was 93 degrees Fahrenheit outside.

But, whatever, things happen, and I understand... but the behavior from the front desk staff was immediately unpleasant and unprofessional. Maybe I'm used to a higher standard of behavior, I don't know, but she was making it sound like it was my fault for booking a hotel room and not asking if the AC worked, which... come on.

This is what I get for trying to save some money. Ended up staying at a much better hotel down the road.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: The bed was quite narrow and against the wall. The room was very minimalist. There is a strange smell in the room, like cigarettes.

title: I don't recommend it.
pos_text: I spent two nights in the hotel and it was absolutely terrible. The bed was broken and uncomfortable, the bathroom had a leak and was uninhabitable, the breakfast was not fresh – even the eggs looked as if they had been cooked overnight – the vegetables were wilted and the bread was dry.
neg_text: I didn't like anything. The only good things were the parking lot and the hotel reception; Otherwise, everything was bad.

title: Exceptional
pos_text: Cleanliness, quietness, amazing breakfast, perfect staff.
neg_text: Damp in the room.

title: Superb
pos_text: Hotel ok, clean, nice staff, good communication.. Breakfast was great! Beautiful selection of food and very tasty.
neg_text: The beds were too soft for my taste, but it wasn't a problem for a 2-night stay.

title: Not worth the money
pos_text: Interesting building. Local bakerd goods were tasty.
neg_text: Bathroom with glass wall is really weird idea. The room was relatively small. Breakfast food selection was limited.

title: I am disappointed
pos_text: Thread
neg_text:
Unfortunately, my stay at this hotel was very disappointing. I found bedbugs in the room, which made a peaceful night's sleep completely impossible. The worst thing was that the staff seemed to be aware of the problem but took no concrete action to solve it. Additionally, there was no possibility of contacting the manager, which only deepened the feeling of helplessness and lack of professionalism.

The lack of appropriate response and concern for the comfort of guests is absolutely unacceptable. I do not recommend this place – instead of rest, I experienced enormous stress and discomfort.

title: The first and last time

neg_text: At the entrance we were greeted by the stench of burning cigarettes, and the sheets on one of the beds were dirty. There was a used condom on the windowsill. After informing the ladies at the reception about the above, there was no reaction, only a request to send a photo to the provided e-mail address. After writing a complaint by email, no response. I will not book again.

title: Not to be repeated. Beautiful on the outside, but less so on the inside.
pos_text: Breakfast was fine.
neg_text: The shower wouldn't close, the shower head was completely loose, and the sofa bed was too sagged to lie on. There was a wedding party going on and we were woken up at 5:20 by a partygoer who couldn't find his room and was banging on our door.

title: Good breakfast, no room service
pos_text: Good location. Very tasty breakfasts. The room is spacious, plenty of space, bright, comfortable beds, fridge and dishes in the room.
neg_text:
The windows could not be opened.
No curtains - the light from the morning was shining in my eyes.
In the bathroom, after a shower, water stands on the floor outside the cabin and there is no way to collect it because there is nothing to use. There is no towel for your feet, which, combined with the stagnant water and the lack of service, makes entering the bathroom disgusting after a while.
Due to the lack of service, you only receive one small bottle of water and one tea per stay. Normal conditions in an apartment, but not in a 3☆ hotel. I never abuse the service, but it is good to be able to ask, for example, towel, water or cleaning the bathroom where there is standing water..
There is only one hand towel for two people.

title: We enjoyed it. Breakfast was delicious.

title: Failed ☹️
neg_text: The hotel remembers the times of the Polish People's Republic. The staff was so scared that they had to hold their tongues to get any information. The price is quite high considering the conditions. The icing on the cake was the information that air conditioning in the room we had had to be paid separately. This is a joke in an age where air conditioning is becoming standard. Breakfast was quite ok, but the coffee machine should have been scrapped long ago. Very long waiting time for the coffee machine to make coffee. It would take a long time to enumerate the disadvantages. In conclusion, never staying at this hotel again.

title: Does not meet the overall standard of the Radisson Blue chain
pos_text: For a hotel of this level - so-so. Limited selection of dishes, practically no choice of sausages, the only pleasant surprise is a set for preparing "Big Tomas".
neg_text: Climate control in the room. It's terrible - it's almost impossible to adjust the temperature - it's impossible to sleep.

title: I do not recommend and I will not come back again

neg_text: The staff seemed to be sitting there as if they were being punished, especially the blonde lady at the reception. The only cleaning ladies are nice and helpful.

title: I do not recommend and will not return.

Aggressive, drunken guests who came to the match at the neighboring stadium. Verbal attacks on people speaking English. Service unresponsive in any way. A drunk man falls down in the hall, in front of the reception desk - the staff does not react in any way.

The rooms are very tight, you can barely walk past the bed. There are no electrical outlets by the bed to connect your phone to charge.

Breakfast expensive in relation to the quality.

The restaurant is open until 10pm. Returning to room ~9pm after the conference, there is really no time to eat something after a quick shower.

neg_text: There is no proper air conditioning and the room windows cannot be opened. It was uncomfortably warm in the room, especially at night.

neg_text:
We stayed at this hotel for the weekend with a friend and we specifically chose it because of the relaxation area – jacuzzi and SPA, which were one of the main reasons for our booking. Unfortunately, the jacuzzi was not working for the entire two days of our stay. No one informed us about this in advance, and when we checked in they only said that "they will fix it tomorrow" – which did not happen.

The room was very cold and I would rate the cleanliness as 2/5 - definitely not at the level of a hotel that costs over PLN 600 per night. Reception service was also poor – 2/5. Upon check-out, we were not given change for 50 PLN, which I only noticed later.

The breakfasts were delicious – that's the only positive aspect of the stay.

We filed a complaint via Booking.com and received a response that the hotel could offer us… a voucher worth PLN 100 to be used for your next booking at the same facility. It is hard to consider it as taking the situation seriously – such a “gesture” after a completely inaccessible jacuzzi and numerous inconveniences it seems simply ridiculous.

We came with the intention of relaxing and unwinding in the wellness area, but we had to look for entertainment outside the hotel. If we had known that it would not be possible to enjoy a holiday of this standard, we would have definitely chosen a cheaper hotel – this one was absolutely not worth the price.

title: Not worth the price, dirty bathroom and terrible service, as if they were working as a punishment, hidden fees
pos_text: The hotel is nice because it is stylish and nicely restored, and that is where the positives of this hotel end
neg_text: Employee approach to customer service. The gentleman working at the reception and the lady behind the bar gave me the impression that they were working there as a punishment. On the first day we were given a room without windows but with the name Anna, just as we wanted. The bathroom seemed to be not cleaned and there were remnants of cosmetics from the previous owner, which is documented in the photos when I asked the receptionist to move or upgrade to another room. He said that these rooms were not available. I was very surprised because the rooms were still available on booking.com, but due to the late hour, we didn't make much of a fuss. Suddenly the next day another employee upgraded us without any problem. When we wanted to use the bar, the lady said the bar was closed, and the gentleman at the reception who checked us in also said the same, even though there were people sitting inside.30 minutes later the bar was suddenly open and I could order a beer when I saw other guests ordering too. Work as punishment? It might be worth training employees. On the first evening we reported a problem with hot water because after one person took a bath it turned out that there was no hot water. The next morning the same problem also appeared and we reported the problem to the reception. On the second night after moving to another room, the problem with the water continued. Sometimes it was hot, sometimes it wasn't. We reported it several times to the reception desk, everyone pretended that everything was fine. The problem only resolved on Monday after check-out, when the receptionist admitted that they were right that they had a problem with the hot water. It's a shame that for two days, none of the employees were able to notice that cold water was running from the tap in every room. The pool area was very nice, but there was no one to ask about massages or additional spa services. Apparently this is a spa and wellness hotel?

title: I don't recommend it.

neg_text: Small room, toilet entrance about 20 cm from the bed, smell of sewage from the bathroom, air conditioning is a lifesaver, but without it the bathroom stinks, limited parking spaces, and despite the parking fee of PLN 40 per night, no parking space was available. Only after some time did we find a space, but it wasn't entirely safe and not in the hotel parking lot, the mattresses were sliding off the beds as if they were a bit too big.

title: Never again
pos_text: The rooms were clean, the location was also good in terms of accessibility.
neg_text:
The rude behavior of the receptionist, who acted as if we were bothering her, everything was a problem and information about the accommodation, e.g. How much and where is breakfast, info about the pool, etc. The highlight was the problem with parking, when the lady told me that they were full and that we should leave the car in a shopping mall about half an hour away on foot or at KFC (where they will tow you away after 2 hours). I explained to the lady that we had found a spot, that we were already standing there, and the cone (which indicates that the spot was occupied) was not there. About ten minutes later, a friend at another reception desk arranged it without any problems.

The pool was small and smelly. According to the instructions on paper and from another receptionist, we are not allowed to take towels from the room to the pool (which seemed logical and normal to me), they are supposedly available at the pool for a deposit. The pool staff completely ignored us and after we pulled him out of watching a video on his computer, he started yelling at us that he didn't have any towels, that maybe we had towels in our room, so what do we want from him?

The bar, which advertises itself as open 24 hours, was no longer staffed at 10 pm. The lady at the reception told us that no one was there, that she would try to call someone... after about half an hour of waiting, the receptionist came to pour us a drink.

Overall, we are absolutely appalled by this hotel, where every employee has different information, everyone is very unpleasant and nothing actually works. We will definitely not return here and I would definitely not recommend staying here to anyone.

After reading the reviews, we decided to stay at this hotel, but unfortunately, it was a real disappointment. In our humble opinion, this property does not have the characteristics of a 4-star hotel; to be generous, I would only give it 3 stars. The furniture and bathrooms are very dated and the rooms are small. The reception staff was very rude both during check in and also when, the next day, we complained because at 4pm. 00 the rooms had not yet been cleaned. The girl at reception arrogantly said that they should prioritise cleaning the rooms needed for tourists checking in, without even apologising for the inconvenience. So if you want to take a shower or rest for an hour in the afternoon, it's better to change hotels. Cleaning only consisted of changing towels and making the bed in a cursory manner. We found the hair we left on the bathroom floor in the same place. The rooms were equipped with a coffee maker and a kettle. It's a shame that we only found the pods on the first day, from the second day onwards nothing could be done, the machines were only there for show. Breakfast left a lot to be desired. Not much selection in the sweets department. Every day only croissants and a small portion of cake. Only two types of jam and in small quantities, not enough for everyone. Nutella or other non-existent chocolate spread. Every day the same variety of savory dishes and bread, not always fresh that day. In short, a big disappointment, nothing to do with the
other 4-star hotels where we stayed in other cities in Poland.

There are no kettles in the rooms to boil tea, nor are there any available downstairs in the hotel lobby, and the mini bar is serviced by the lady at the reception, who will either come and serve you if she wants, or she won't. title: Never again…
pos_text:

neg_text: Hotel two days before arrival (date is early December) he sent information that they had a special occasion - a company Christmas Eve party. If I were to change the location, hotel prices at that time for such a close date cost at least PLN 500 per night (ignoring the problem of availability on weekends) - I come to Wrocław alone to study. I arrived before 10pm, tired after a full day of education. I think I spent over 20 minutes at the reception, even though I had already provided all the information on booking - invoice details, phone number, and I don't know what else the lady wrote there. I say they already have my number, she says she doesn't have a number. And the hotel called me 2 hours earlier... Typing speed slower than slow. She was supposed to send me the invoice by email right away. The music was blaring beneath me all night long, and the receptionist refused to move my room to a quieter area, even though there was a place on the opposite side of the room. At 4:30 I was woken up by the screams of a couple making love. And I don't think they were very loud at all, but the walls are very thin. A great night as before the next morning classes, waking up at 6:30. The bathroom was recently renovated, but the shower head was probably 20 years old. Hair stuck to the shower walls. But only 4. As if that wasn't enough, the hotel doesn't know how to issue invoices. I waited almost 3 weeks. There was another problem because they were supposedly issuing a correction for a week because someone made a mistake. They responded to messages with a delay of several days. And the atmosphere is a bit like the PRL. All together: a tragedy in its entirety.

title: Stay with obstacles
pos_text:
The breakfast was plentiful and good. The location is very central. Just a few hundred meters to the market square.
The room was very large, as was the bathroom, and both were very clean. The heating worked well too.
neg_text:
The key card didn't work. The parking garage, which had spaces for hotel guests, was located on a one-way street where the hotel was also situated. However, the parking garage was a few meters in front of the hotel, so you couldn't turn around and drive straight in, but had to go forward through half the city and make a loop to get into the one-way street. For me, it happened at night and in the rain, so it was very tiring. Then there were several entrances to parking garages next to each other. The description of the parking lot was only in Polish. I encountered several cars reversing out of the various underground parking garages, all looking for the right spot, just like me. The whole thing took me almost 2 hours. It was said to be a secure parking garage, but I didn't even need the key because everything was always open.
Then I decided to stay another day and asked if there was a room available, perhaps even mine? The receptionist said no. Only one with a lower standard, with only one bed. I said it was no problem, since I was traveling alone anyway. Then she said that I would have to check out of my current room and could only check back in at 3 pm, and that I would have to wait until then. That made no sense to me, of course, as I wanted to see the city during the day. There was absolutely no willingness to store my suitcases for that long. Overall, both receptionists were neither friendly nor helpful.

title: Poor

However, the security guard or another person rummaged through my things and called the police because they found them.suspicious pills", what gives you the right to rummage through other people's things? The tablets were repacked in a plastic bag because it was more convenient for me. The police arrived, they knew the medications - because they were regular tablets, not some powder, I showed them the prescriptions and the whole intervention lasted 5 minutes. However, calling the police prevented me from taking the medicine and I lost about an hour or more of time. The police were wondering whether to give the receptionist a ticket for an unjustified call (as we were sitting in the room with the policemen), but they refrained from doing so. And now the best part - the wireless headphones were missing from the belongings, when I mentioned this, the security guard was told to check the room and suddenly they were found in 20 seconds...

The deluxe room with a bathroom behind the glass visible from the entire room is a unique solution, I was glad I was alone.
The guy at the reception simply advised against using the hotel's services, and when I mentioned that I was leaving at 4 a.m., paying for a room with breakfast, he said it was too late to order a lunchbox. I probably won't come back. 5 stars seems like a joke for this level of service.

title: If I may advise, for your stay in Wroclaw, definitely choose another hotel for the sake of your health.
neg_text:
Upon arriving at the hotel, I was very unpleasantly surprised that the window in the room could not be opened. The temperature in the room was about 25 degrees. After asking at the reception, I was told that this was to prevent guests from jumping out of the window and that I should use the air conditioning. I was unable to adjust the air conditioning to lower the temperature, instead it reached 30 degrees within a few minutes. After further inquiry at the reception, I was told that the air conditioning cannot be set to cooling during the winter months, only to heating. When I asked (in English) how I could lower the temperature in the room, as it was impossible to sleep peacefully at 28 degrees, the receptionist replied that we were in Poland, and therefore she would only speak Polish to me. However, within the next few sentences, she smoothly switched to Ukrainian, probably because I couldn't understand her. Still, she didn't give me any advice.
I find the receptionist's approach completely unprofessional and discouraging.
She is probably employed there to discourage guests from asking unpleasant questions at the reception.
The sleep at night was very restless, it was impossible to breathe peacefully, and in the morning I woke up with a severe headache. There was no question of rest.
When I realize that the room has no ventilation, and the air in the room has certainly been the same for the last few years, I can be glad that I didn't end up with some serious problem in the care of healthcare professionals. This can be a very serious problem for allergy sufferers.
I have stayed in about 16 hotels across Europe over the last two years, but the experience at this hotel is clearly the worst.

It was by far the worst nights sleep I have ever had! Then the next morning I thought I would get up and go for breakfast and sit in the spa hoping my room would get cleaned and I could then go back to sleep … return back to room at 4pm to still not have been cleaned , had to call reception over-wise I don’t think it would of been done. Very disappointed with the whole place and will not be returning. Didn’t even get a message back from the hotel to acknowledge the whole situation or offer any sort of solution. Waited 24 hours for a message and didn’t get it so here I am … my advice for any one going to stay here find out if they are having a party etc and if they are do not stay there.

title: Next time I won't choose Radisson, in Wrocław or anywhere else
pos_text: Location - close to the old town
neg_text:
1. I asked to reserve a parking space - I received an answer that they cannot reserve it, but it costs PLN 110 per day.
2. After entering the room, I decided I would like a beer - I looked into the bar - empty. The lady at the reception explained that due to the pandemic, they removed drinks from the mini-bar and will probably not return them - company policy.
3. If there's no beer, I'll drink water, whatever. There is no water either, there is a carafe and a message to look for a dispenser in the corridor and pour some. Apparently they want to be ecological and have given up using plastic. They could have given it in a glass bottle. Apparently Radisson's policy too.
4. And to top it all off, there's a cloth bag on the bed with a story about how to skip cleaning the room because it's eco-friendly.
RADISSON! I have a few more ideas for you: convince your customers not to flush or wash themselves, change bed linen and towels for every third customer - you will be even more eco-friendly!!!
5. The best was at the end. When checking out, I told the lady at the reception about the above observations, to which she replied that she fully understood me. When I said I was going to share this in my hotel review, she immediately offered me a discount for my stay and asked me not to. It seems that they are afraid of bad reviews, they have a prepared response to individual cases in the form of a bribe, but they do not intend to change it systematically.
To sum up, it is not worth paying 510 for this pseudo ***** hotel. I will do that next time

Delicious coffee.Efficient service.

Restaurant "Dwór Polski" has an entrance from the Market Square. Great location, service and delicious dishes. I recommend!!
neg_text:
The windows of my room faced the street. Terrible noise until morning - people, motorbikes and cars. After my hike in the Karkonosze Mountains I couldn't rest.
When I opened the window it fell off its hinges. On the floor there is a carpet that has seen its glory days. The dark brown furniture is meant to resemble a manor house. They are worn out.
I do not recommend a room with windows facing the street during the holidays.

I booked this hotel solely for the pool, gym and sauna.
On the day of arrival, they initially refused to give me a robe, citing the fact that the hotel was overbooked and all the robes had already been taken for today, so come back tomorrow. then they said that I had a cheap room, since it was for one person. ( I don't understand anything here at all, is it cheaper because it's for one person) or don't all room categories include a robe in a hotel with a pool?
An hour later, another extremely nice young man, without any questions, arranged for two robes to be delivered to my room in 15 minutes.
But, as it turned out, that day the pool had very cold water, and the sauna was practically not heating. The gym was completely closed. There were 3 sun loungers, which is very few. On the second day, the pool became a little warmer, but still uncomfortable, and the sauna was even colder. One sun lounger was already broken and there were 2 left. Then I just got sick, I assume it was because of the hypothermia in their “spa” and for the third day I didn’t go anywhere, and there was no point.
Also, on the second day, there were no towels in the locker room at this spa and there was no one at the spa reception desk to ask for anything.
If you don't need all that, it's a normal average hotel in the city center with good breakfasts.

Fifthly - a nice lighting effect in the bathroom - the wall was made of glass doors leading to a balcony shared by several rooms, but .... matte foil on one of the windows was carelessly glued, which meant that when standing on the balcony in the evening, with the bathroom light on - the person standing on the balcony (I remind you of the shared balcony) has an excellent view of the person using the bathroom and unless you are an exhibitionist, this is very uncomfortable.

title: Long wait at check-in
pos_text: Comfortable room, excellent breakfast
neg_text:
1. I arrived at the hotel at 12 noon on the day of arrival. I was asked at reception to wait until 3 pm. Only then would check-in be possible and the room ready for occupancy.
Okay, I came back at 2 pm after a walk through the city. Once again, I was told to come back at 3 pm.

Is it really too much to ask that rooms be prepared a little earlier and not be rigidly insisted upon by the fixed time?

So I had to wait another hour in the lobby until the room was finally ready. Then only after repeated requests at reception. The journey had been tiring, and all they wanted to do was relax a bit in their room.

It might only take 20 minutes to prepare a single room.

The organization is poor in this regard.
I will arrive after 3 pm on my next visit.

The Scandic is not some fly-by-night hotel, but a large, reputable hotel.

2. When I checked out, the young man at reception wanted to charge me separately for breakfast, even though it was already included in the package and I had already paid. The error has been resolved. The employee did not apologize, which I had hoped for.

Overall, I had the impression that Polish guests were treated more kindly and courteously than Germans.

title: Nice hotel, but in summer!
pos_text: Very nice hotel. A big plus was the cleanliness, the swimming pool and the location, which perfectly suited my needs during my stay. I also appreciate the proximity of shops and restaurants. This is the second time I have stayed at the hotel; my previous visit took place during the summer and I rated it very highly. Unfortunately, this time I have to lower my rating.
neg_text: The room was a real sauna. Air conditioning (according to the information on the plate located next to the control panel) in winter it only works in heating mode and despite being set to the lowest possible 16°C, it only heated the already warm room, and the window in the room cannot be opened or tilted. This is quite a surprise considering the amount you have to pay per night. Unfortunately, this made it difficult to fall asleep and caused constant waking up, which significantly affected the comfort of the stay - I wanted to run away from the room. If I decide to stay again, it will only be in the summer; however, in the winter I will look for another hotel with working air conditioning.

title: Moderately successful.
pos_text: Overall, a nice hotel and my rating would be completely positive if it weren't for a coincidence. I arrived in Wrocław in the evening on the day when the Polish national football team was playing a match against Portugal. When I was going to Wrocław, I was hoping that I would be able to watch the match in the hotel - on the TV in the room or at least in the hotel restaurant. It turned out that there were no TVP channels in the entire hotel, so there was no way to watch the match. Let us add that this hotel is somehow connected with the Śląsk Wrocław football club! I ended up having to go to the city center (it's about a 20-minute tram ride) and find a pub there, where I watched most of the match - because unfortunately I didn't make it in time for the start. Unfortunately, the bad taste remained - plus additional night trips around the city in the rain, which I had not planned.
neg_text: Apart from that, everything was OK.
